Since $MRVL was up +32%, let me introduce you Taiwan’s Most Direct $MRVL related Stock: LuxNet Corporation 華星光（4979):

Marvell’s push into high-speed Optical DSPs and silicon photonics/CPO solutions for AI data centers has created clear demand for supporting photonic components. LuxNet stands out because of its specific role and long-term relationship with Marvell.

Here’s why the linkage is considered particularly direct:

1.  Critical Photonic Component Supplier
 LuxNet produces InP-based CW Lasers, which serve as the essential external light source in silicon photonics and CPO architectures. These lasers are a key part of enabling Marvell’s advanced Optical DSP platforms.

2.  Long-Standing Partnership The relationship goes back to Marvell’s acquisition of Inphi. 

LuxNet has been a manufacturing and development partner for Marvell’s high-speed optical transceiver modules and continues to work with the company on next-generation solutions.

3.  Frequently Cited in Market Coverage
 When Marvell reports progress in optical or silicon photonics, or when Nvidia deepens cooperation with Marvell, LuxNet is regularly included in analyst and media lists of the most direct Taiwan beneficiaries.
 Example: Recent coverage on Nvidia-Marvell silicon photonics collaboration highlighted LuxNet as part of the Taiwan optical supply chain that stands to benefit.
4.  Higher Specificity Than Most Peers Unlike broader semiconductor or foundry plays, LuxNet’s involvement is more component-specific and tied directly to Marvell’s optical product roadmap, making the supply chain connection more visible and targeted.

This combination of technical role, historical partnership, and consistent market recognition is why LuxNet is often viewed as one of the clearest $MRVL supply chain exposures in Taiwan.

text:
Wind Financial Morning Post: June 3, 2026

Market Brief

The State Council issues the "15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization"

China achieves significant breakthrough in optical communication technology

Shanghai establishes a new landmark for the global asset management center: scale to reach RMB 5.5 trillion by 2030

Prospects for U.S.-Iran negotiations remain uncertain, while applications for transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz have opened

The United States lowers import tariffs on selected steel, aluminum, and copper products, and plans to impose a new 25% tariff on Brazil

Global stock markets rose Tuesday, with U.S. markets hitting new highs and South Korea’s market entering the world’s top six largest securities markets

Tiger Brokers: Suspends new position opening and additional positioning for existing investor accounts in all domestic Chinese instruments effective June 12

Microsoft releases seven new AI models to create an integrated “thinking + programming” agent ecosystem

Top Stories

Shanghai has unveiled 21 measures to deepen its construction as a global asset management center, aiming for total asset management scale of 5.4 trillion yuan by 2030, accounting for one-third of the national total. The city will support listings and mergers of technology firms with strong innovation profiles; accelerate the launch of LNG futures and options; prepare for power and computing power futures; promote the listing of Star 50 Index, Shenzhen 100 Index, ChiNext Index futures and options, and government bond options; enhance mechanisms such as “Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect,” “China-Europe Connect,” and “Bond Connect”; and steadily expand institutional openness in the gold market.

Tiger Brokers announced that, to comply with regulatory requirements during the cross-border securities business consolidation period, it will adjust services for existing investor accounts in mainland China starting June 12. Measures include suspending new positions and additional purchases across all securities products, permitting only sell and close-out transactions; and halting inbound fund transfers while maintaining outbound transfer functionality. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $155 million, up 26.3% year-over-year, and operating profit of $48 million, up 17.5%.

According to Iranian sources, the system for applying for vessel transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz has been fully opened, allowing global shipowners and captains to submit applications. Upon approval, vessels will receive transit authorization. Iranian media reported that information exchanges between Iran and the U.S. have been suspended for several days, despite Western media and officials claiming ongoing dialogue. U.S. President Trump stated that talks with Iran continue. U.S. Secretary of State Rubio affirmed that negotiations are still underway but expressed uncertainty over a potential agreement.

Microsoft unveiled seven new AI models at the Build 2026 developer conference, covering full-stack capabilities in reasoning, coding, vision, speech, and multimodal functions. Its flagship reasoning model, MAI-Thinking-1, matched Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 4.6 on coding benchmarks. The 5-billion-parameter coding model, MAI-Code-1-Flash, targets the GitHub ecosystem. Microsoft also introduced Web IQ, a search engine defined as a “network intelligence layer for AI agents.”

China Macro & Policy

The State Council issued the “15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization,” outlining major projects including high-standard farmland construction and cold-chain logistics facilities. The plan promotes diversified food development and the “AI+” agriculture initiative, and identifies key policy measures to cultivate emerging and future industries in agriculture.

National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) Director Zheng Zhijie convened a meeting with state-owned enterprises to discuss deepening SOE reform, advancing the national unified market, promoting green and low-carbon development, and ensuring energy security. Zheng emphasized enhancing the technological innovation, industrial control, and security support functions of state-owned enterprises.

The Ministry of Finance recently allocated 99.9 billion yuan in childcare subsidies for 2026, a 10.6% increase from last year, to support local governments in providing subsidies to eligible infants. Total fiscal subsidies for the year are projected to reach approximately 110 billion yuan.

The People’s Bank of China reported that in May, standing lending facility (SLF) net injections amounted to 900 million yuan, pledged supplementary lending (PSL) net withdrawals totaled 154.5 billion yuan, and other structural monetary policy tools net injected 112.9 billion yuan; open market treasury purchases resulted in a net injection of 50 billion yuan.

China’s first online tax-refund-for-overseas-tourists store has officially launched in Beijing, alongside the simultaneous activation of centralized tax-refund points in the city’s hotel sector. Overseas visitors can now process tax refunds directly at hotels after online purchases, expanding the scope of tax-refund services.

China Equities Markets

A-share markets climbed modestly, with the SSE Index rising 0.43% to 4,075.10, the Shenzhen Component Index up 1.63%, and the ChiNext Index jumping 2.66%. The Wind All China Index gained 0.75%, with total market turnover reaching 2.81 trillion yuan. The AI computing hardware supply chain strengthened again, with CPO and optical fiber sectors surging. Components, AI PCs, PCBs, and MLCC concepts advanced. Coal stocks maintained strength, while film and theater, and short-form video game concepts declined.

The Hang Seng Index closed up 2.52% at 26,038.32, the Hang Seng Tech Index surged 4.72%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index rose 3%. Technology stocks rallied broadly, with Tencent Holdings soaring over 10%. New energy vehicles and non-ferrous metals led gains, while AI applications and coal stocks declined. Net inflows via the Southbound Stock Connect totaled 2.2 billion Hong Kong dollars, with Tencent Holdings experiencing 2.1 billion Hong Kong dollars in net outflows.

Fund managers led by Jin Zicai announced that to ensure stable fund operations and protect investor interests, subscription limits for multiple funds — including Caichong Growth Optimum, Caichong Value Momentum, Caichong Integrated Circuit Industry, and Caichong Quality Selection — have been lowered to 1,000 yuan.

The Shanghai Stock Exchange website shows that Yushu Technology’s STAR Market IPO status has changed to “submitted for registration.” Yushu Technology’s market director Huang Jiawei revealed that the company’s new-generation humanoid robot reference design, H2 Plus, developed jointly with NVIDIA, will launch in the second half of the year.

China Industry News

China has achieved a major breakthrough in optical communications. The world’s first S+C+L triple-band (short, conventional, and long wavelength) ultra-low-loss multi-core optical cable line has been officially commissioned in Qingdao, Shandong. The single-fiber capacity exceeds five times that of traditional fibers, fully meeting future demands for AI computing and terabit-per-second ultra-high-speed transmission.

Four ministries have launched the “Sword Net 2026” campaign to combat online copyright infringement, with a focus on AI-related copyright issues. The initiative targets violations such as “remixing,” “plagiarism,” and deepfake content in large model training datasets.

According to Centaline Property Research, 141,000 second-hand residential units were sold in 20 cities in May, up 19.3% year-over-year, with the growth rate expanding 6.3 percentage points from April. Beijing and Shanghai recorded 16,000 and 28,000 second-hand home sales respectively, up 12.1% and 30.9% year-over-year, marking the highest May levels in nearly five years. The average second-hand home price across 100 cities fell 0.32% month-over-month, while new home prices rose 0.16%.

Guangzhou’s Nansha District unveiled “Ten Measures” for real estate policy, launching an “old-home-for-new-home” initiative where proceeds from selling an old property can be directly applied toward a new purchase. A subsidy of 1% of the new home’s total price — capped at 20,000 yuan per unit — will be provided, and this can be stacked with municipal subsidies.

The China Passenger Car Association estimated that domestic new energy passenger vehicle wholesale sales reached 1.36 million units in May, up over 11% year-over-year and month-over-month. According to the China Automobile Dealers Association, the automobile consumption index stood at 81 in May, slightly higher than April, suggesting a continued steady upward trend in overall sales for June.

Fintech

Shanghai’s guidelines on deepening its global asset management center status encourage asset managers to leverage AI, blockchain, and big data technologies to accelerate intelligent investment research and advisory services. The city will support the establishment of AI application pilot bases for asset management.

The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular requiring licensed institutions — particularly online brokers and virtual asset platforms — to strengthen cybersecurity measures to counter emerging threats driven by advanced AI models.

Corporate News & Updates

Sources indicate Tencent has completed prototype testing of its WeChat AI agent and will initiate compliance review procedures as early as this month. Users will be able to use the agent to trigger hundreds of mini-programs for daily tasks such as food ordering. The project has been designated the highest strategic priority. Tencent Cloud has reduced pricing for the DeepSeek-V4 series models by up to 97.5%.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang stated that agent-based AI will fundamentally reshape all AI applications and represent the most important computing transformation over the next decade. Huang also said Marvell Technology will become the next trillion-dollar company. NVIDIA announced full-scale mass production of its NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet silicon photonics technology based on CPO.

Samsung Electronics unveiled its eighth-generation high-bandwidth memory chip, HBM5, and publicly introduced its HPB cooling solution for the first time. Chief Technology Officer Jae-Hyung Song confirmed that HPB technology has been validated on HBM4E chips and will be commercially deployed with HBM5.

Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, in his Computex 2026 keynote, said agentic AI is reshaping computing demand, and future AI infrastructure will enter a heterogeneous era of co-development among CPUs, GPUs, ASICs, and custom chips. Demand for CPUs in data centers is expected to re-enter a growth cycle.

SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won said the global shortage of memory chips may persist until 2030, and the company is increasing capital expenditures to bridge the supply-demand gap. SK Hynix plans to double its wafer capacity over the next five years.

ByteDance’s DouBao large model is expected to launch paid content in late June, with plans to integrate e-commerce functions in the third quarter to drive traffic to Douyin Mall. Longer-term operational strategies are not ruled out.

Anthropic announced the expansion of its Glasswing initiative to 150 new partner organizations across more than 15 countries. These institutions will use the Mythos tool to identify software vulnerabilities. New partners span energy, water, healthcare, telecommunications, and hardware industries.

OpenAI released a suite of new plugins for its Codex agent, covering public equity investment, banking, and sales use cases, and plans to introduce legal and corporate finance functions soon. OpenAI also intends to integrate Codex into ChatGPT.

Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen model launched Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal large model capable of understanding images and videos, performing deep reasoning, self-programming, tool invocation, validation, and autonomous iteration. It can complete complex, long-horizon tasks such as replicating smartphone apps and desktop software with a single command.

Global Macro & Policy

U.S. President Trump signed an executive order temporarily adjusting import tariffs on certain steel, aluminum, and copper products, effective June 8 through December 31, 2027. Import tariffs on harvesters and other agricultural machinery will be reduced from 25% to 15%, and the domestic content threshold for imported steel, aluminum, and copper will be lowered from 95% to 85%.

The United States plans to impose 25% punitive tariffs on a range of Brazilian imports, including electronic payment services, preferential tariff treatment, intellectual property protections, and ethanol market access. Exemptions include beef, coffee, rare earth minerals, aircraft and aircraft parts, crude oil, fertilizers, and numerous fruits and nuts.

The Canadian government has proposed to the U.S. and Mexico that the USMCA be renewed for 16 years to avoid triggering the agreement’s mandatory annual review process. Under USMCA provisions, the three nations are scheduled to conduct their first joint review in July. The agreement, signed in 2018 and effective since July 2020, is set to expire in 2036.

U.S. officials are reportedly discussing whether to deploy nuclear weapons in additional NATO European countries. The U.S. already deploys nuclear-capable bombers in Belgium, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Turkey, and the UK, and has signaled openness to additional deployments.

Hawkish voices within the Federal Reserve are intensifying. Cleveland Fed President Harker said while maintaining current rates remains appropriate, persistent inflation data may soon necessitate policy action — including potential further rate hikes — to counter elevated inflation risks.

Labor market resilience continues to bolster Fed hawkish expectations. U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data showed job openings rose to 7.62 million in April, the highest level in two years and well above the 6.87 million forecast. Layoffs fell to 1.692 million, significantly below expectations.

The European Union plans to impose strict eligibility criteria for cloud computing services in key public procurement projects, potentially excluding Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. The proposal aims to reduce EU dependence on U.S. tech products and promote domestic industry development.

Eurostat preliminary data showed euro area CPI rose 3.2% year-over-year in May, up from 3% in April, reinforcing market expectations of a modest ECB rate hike in June.

South Korea’s central bank is poised to raise rates. Statistics Korea reported that May CPI rose 3.1% year-over-year, the largest increase since March 2024. Core CPI increased 2.5%, the highest since February 2024.

The World Meteorological Organization released a report indicating most climate models predict this El Niño will develop into at least a moderate event and may intensify further. Seasonal forecasts suggest most regions will experience above-average temperatures in the coming months.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. major indices closed slightly higher: the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.45% to 51,307.79, the S&P 500 gained 0.13% to 7,609.78, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.03% to 27,093.90 — all closing at record highs. Cisco rose over 5%, Caterpillar gained more than 5%, leading the Dow. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index declined 1.05%, with Microsoft down over 4% and Google down nearly 4%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 1.83%, with Canadian Solar up over 12% and Hesai Group up over 8%. Traders closely monitored developments in U.S.-Iran relations and major tech stock performance.

European indices closed higher: Germany’s DAX rose 0.48% to 25,124.17, France’s CAC 40 gained 0.77% to 8,209.09, and the UK’s FTSE 100 climbed 0.33% to 10,373.51. Progress in ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the U.S. lifted risk appetite, but Brent crude breached $95 per barrel, maintaining upward pressure on inflation expectations.

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed. South Korea’s KOSPI swung sharply — initially surging before plunging more than 3% — and ultimately closed up 0.15% at 8,801.49, setting a new record. South Korea’s total market capitalization surpassed India’s, becoming the world’s sixth-largest stock market. Japan’s Nikkei 225 fell 0.3% to 66,734.24. India’s SENSEX rose 0.52% to 74,649.84.

Andrew Left, founder of the renowned short-selling firm Citron Research, was convicted by a U.S. court of stock manipulation and may face decades in prison. The case could mark a landmark moment for short-sellers globally and reshape regulatory boundaries for short reports and market commentary.

Data from India’s National Securities Depository Limited showed that as of June 1, foreign institutional investors’ net investment in Indian equities totaled 7.3 trillion rupees — the lowest since 2016. Global fund ownership in Indian equities has declined from nearly 20% a decade ago to 15% today.

Bonds

China’s interbank bond market traded in a narrow range, with yields on major government bonds moving slightly in different directions. Treasury futures rallied slightly at the close, with the 10-year contract up 0.06%. Liquidity conditions returned to ample levels as the PBOC conducted 200 million yuan in reverse repo operations, resulting in a net withdrawal of 248.8 billion yuan.

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed: the 2-year yield rose 1.24 basis points to 4.039%, the 3-year yield rose 0.59 basis points to 4.085%, the 5-year yield held steady at 4.163%, the 10-year yield fell 1.18 basis points to 4.439%, and the 30-year yield declined 1.21 basis points to 4.957%.

Commodities

U.S. crude oil futures rose 1.33% to $93.39 per barrel; Brent crude gained 1.01% to $95.94 per barrel. U.S. API crude inventories fell sharply, exceeding market expectations, while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East — including Iran’s suspension of U.S.-Iran talks and its plan to block the Strait of Hormuz — heightened concerns over supply disruptions. The strait handles roughly 20% of global oil shipments, and global crude inventories remain at low levels, jointly supporting higher prices.

International precious metals futures generally rose: COMEX gold futures gained 0.29% to $4,519.20 per ounce, and COMEX silver futures rose 0.25% to $75.44 per ounce. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East lifted safe-haven demand, while rising U.S. and European rate expectations weighed on market sentiment, creating a tug-of-war.

Most base metals on the LME rose: LME tin climbed 2.88% to $58,280 per ton, LME lead gained 1.52% to $2,037 per ton, LME zinc rose 1.48% to $3,631 per ton, LME aluminum advanced 1.20% to $3,760.50 per ton, LME copper increased 1.10% to $13,983.50 per ton, while LME nickel edged down 0.19% to $19,215 per ton.

The European Central Bank reported that as of the end of 2025, gold accounted for 27% of global official reserve assets, surpassing U.S. Treasuries, which fell to 22%. Gold has become the world’s largest official reserve asset.

Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) revealed plans to construct a refined products pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also exploring a new east-west crude oil trunk line that would allow other Gulf producers to route exports through UAE territory, avoiding the strait.

FX & Currency Markets

Onshore renminbi closed at 6.7622 against the U.S. dollar at 16:30, up 28 basis points from the previous close, and settled at 6.7650 in the night session. The PBOC’s mid-rate was set at 6.8187, a 20-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

At the close of New York trading, the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.03% to 99.22. Non-U.S. currencies were mixed: the euro fell 0.01% to 1.1631, the pound rose 0.07% to 1.3464, the Australian dollar rose 0.27% to 0.7180, the dollar rose 0.16% against the yen to 159.9295, the dollar fell 0.01% against the Canadian dollar to 1.3839, the dollar rose 0.08% against the Swiss franc to 0.7873, and the offshore renminbi gained 23 basis points to 6.7629.

Japan’s authorities issued a strong signal after record-sized foreign exchange interventions proved ineffective. Finance Minister Kato said the government stands ready to take appropriate action on exchange rates if necessary.

Key Economic Events

21:45 EDT, Jun 2: China S&P Global Services/Composite PMI for May

09:00 EDT, Jun 3: Fed Governor Barr participates in dialogue at the 2026 Community Bankers Association Industry Forum

US Treasury Secretary Bessent to testify before Senate on budget issues

The 2026 St. Petersburg International Economic Forum to be held Jun 3-6

The 19th SNEC-Shanghai Photovoltaic Exhibition to be held Jun 3-5

The 2026 Shanghai International Semiconductor Technology Conference & Exhibition to be held

Huawei Smart PV 2026 Strategy & New Product Launch

Market Brief

The State Council issues the "15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization"

China achieves significant breakthrough in optical communication technology

Shanghai establishes a new landmark for the global asset management center: scale to reach RMB 5.5 trillion by 2030

Prospects for U.S.-Iran negotiations remain uncertain, while applications for transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz have opened

The United States lowers import tariffs on selected steel, aluminum, and copper products, and plans to impose a new 25% tariff on Brazil

Global stock markets rose Tuesday, with U.S. markets hitting new highs and South Korea’s market entering the world’s top six largest securities markets

Tiger Brokers: Suspends new position opening and additional positioning for existing investor accounts in all domestic Chinese instruments effective June 12

Microsoft releases seven new AI models to create an integrated “thinking + programming” agent ecosystem

Top Stories

Shanghai has unveiled 21 measures to deepen its construction as a global asset management center, aiming for total asset management scale of 5.4 trillion yuan by 2030, accounting for one-third of the national total. The city will support listings and mergers of technology firms with strong innovation profiles; accelerate the launch of LNG futures and options; prepare for power and computing power futures; promote the listing of Star 50 Index, Shenzhen 100 Index, ChiNext Index futures and options, and government bond options; enhance mechanisms such as “Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect,” “China-Europe Connect,” and “Bond Connect”; and steadily expand institutional openness in the gold market.

Tiger Brokers announced that, to comply with regulatory requirements during the cross-border securities business consolidation period, it will adjust services for existing investor accounts in mainland China starting June 12. Measures include suspending new positions and additional purchases across all securities products, permitting only sell and close-out transactions; and halting inbound fund transfers while maintaining outbound transfer functionality. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $155 million, up 26.3% year-over-year, and operating profit of $48 million, up 17.5%.

According to Iranian sources, the system for applying for vessel transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz has been fully opened, allowing global shipowners and captains to submit applications. Upon approval, vessels will receive transit authorization. Iranian media reported that information exchanges between Iran and the U.S. have been suspended for several days, despite Western media and officials claiming ongoing dialogue. U.S. President Trump stated that talks with Iran continue. U.S. Secretary of State Rubio affirmed that negotiations are still underway but expressed uncertainty over a potential agreement.

Microsoft unveiled seven new AI models at the Build 2026 developer conference, covering full-stack capabilities in reasoning, coding, vision, speech, and multimodal functions. Its flagship reasoning model, MAI-Thinking-1, matched Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 4.6 on coding benchmarks. The 5-billion-parameter coding model, MAI-Code-1-Flash, targets the GitHub ecosystem. Microsoft also introduced Web IQ, a search engine defined as a “network intelligence layer for AI agents.”

China Macro & Policy

The State Council issued the “15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization,” outlining major projects including high-standard farmland construction and cold-chain logistics facilities. The plan promotes diversified food development and the “AI+” agriculture initiative, and identifies key policy measures to cultivate emerging and future industries in agriculture.

National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) Director Zheng Zhijie convened a meeting with state-owned enterprises to discuss deepening SOE reform, advancing the national unified market, promoting green and low-carbon development, and ensuring energy security. Zheng emphasized enhancing the technological innovation, industrial control, and security support functions of state-owned enterprises.

The Ministry of Finance recently allocated 99.9 billion yuan in childcare subsidies for 2026, a 10.6% increase from last year, to support local governments in providing subsidies to eligible infants. Total fiscal subsidies for the year are projected to reach approximately 110 billion yuan.

The People’s Bank of China reported that in May, standing lending facility (SLF) net injections amounted to 900 million yuan, pledged supplementary lending (PSL) net withdrawals totaled 154.5 billion yuan, and other structural monetary policy tools net injected 112.9 billion yuan; open market treasury purchases resulted in a net injection of 50 billion yuan.

China’s first online tax-refund-for-overseas-tourists store has officially launched in Beijing, alongside the simultaneous activation of centralized tax-refund points in the city’s hotel sector. Overseas visitors can now process tax refunds directly at hotels after online purchases, expanding the scope of tax-refund services.

China Equities Markets

A-share markets climbed modestly, with the SSE Index rising 0.43% to 4,075.10, the Shenzhen Component Index up 1.63%, and the ChiNext Index jumping 2.66%. The Wind All China Index gained 0.75%, with total market turnover reaching 2.81 trillion yuan. The AI computing hardware supply chain strengthened again, with CPO and optical fiber sectors surging. Components, AI PCs, PCBs, and MLCC concepts advanced. Coal stocks maintained strength, while film and theater, and short-form video game concepts declined.

The Hang Seng Index closed up 2.52% at 26,038.32, the Hang Seng Tech Index surged 4.72%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index rose 3%. Technology stocks rallied broadly, with Tencent Holdings soaring over 10%. New energy vehicles and non-ferrous metals led gains, while AI applications and coal stocks declined. Net inflows via the Southbound Stock Connect totaled 2.2 billion Hong Kong dollars, with Tencent Holdings experiencing 2.1 billion Hong Kong dollars in net outflows.

Fund managers led by Jin Zicai announced that to ensure stable fund operations and protect investor interests, subscription limits for multiple funds — including Caichong Growth Optimum, Caichong Value Momentum, Caichong Integrated Circuit Industry, and Caichong Quality Selection — have been lowered to 1,000 yuan.

The Shanghai Stock Exchange website shows that Yushu Technology’s STAR Market IPO status has changed to “submitted for registration.” Yushu Technology’s market director Huang Jiawei revealed that the company’s new-generation humanoid robot reference design, H2 Plus, developed jointly with NVIDIA, will launch in the second half of the year.

China Industry News

China has achieved a major breakthrough in optical communications. The world’s first S+C+L triple-band (short, conventional, and long wavelength) ultra-low-loss multi-core optical cable line has been officially commissioned in Qingdao, Shandong. The single-fiber capacity exceeds five times that of traditional fibers, fully meeting future demands for AI computing and terabit-per-second ultra-high-speed transmission.

Four ministries have launched the “Sword Net 2026” campaign to combat online copyright infringement, with a focus on AI-related copyright issues. The initiative targets violations such as “remixing,” “plagiarism,” and deepfake content in large model training datasets.

According to Centaline Property Research, 141,000 second-hand residential units were sold in 20 cities in May, up 19.3% year-over-year, with the growth rate expanding 6.3 percentage points from April. Beijing and Shanghai recorded 16,000 and 28,000 second-hand home sales respectively, up 12.1% and 30.9% year-over-year, marking the highest May levels in nearly five years. The average second-hand home price across 100 cities fell 0.32% month-over-month, while new home prices rose 0.16%.

Guangzhou’s Nansha District unveiled “Ten Measures” for real estate policy, launching an “old-home-for-new-home” initiative where proceeds from selling an old property can be directly applied toward a new purchase. A subsidy of 1% of the new home’s total price — capped at 20,000 yuan per unit — will be provided, and this can be stacked with municipal subsidies.

The China Passenger Car Association estimated that domestic new energy passenger vehicle wholesale sales reached 1.36 million units in May, up over 11% year-over-year and month-over-month. According to the China Automobile Dealers Association, the automobile consumption index stood at 81 in May, slightly higher than April, suggesting a continued steady upward trend in overall sales for June.

Fintech

Shanghai’s guidelines on deepening its global asset management center status encourage asset managers to leverage AI, blockchain, and big data technologies to accelerate intelligent investment research and advisory services. The city will support the establishment of AI application pilot bases for asset management.

The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular requiring licensed institutions — particularly online brokers and virtual asset platforms — to strengthen cybersecurity measures to counter emerging threats driven by advanced AI models.

Corporate News & Updates

Sources indicate Tencent has completed prototype testing of its WeChat AI agent and will initiate compliance review procedures as early as this month. Users will be able to use the agent to trigger hundreds of mini-programs for daily tasks such as food ordering. The project has been designated the highest strategic priority. Tencent Cloud has reduced pricing for the DeepSeek-V4 series models by up to 97.5%.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang stated that agent-based AI will fundamentally reshape all AI applications and represent the most important computing transformation over the next decade. Huang also said Marvell Technology will become the next trillion-dollar company. NVIDIA announced full-scale mass production of its NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet silicon photonics technology based on CPO.

Samsung Electronics unveiled its eighth-generation high-bandwidth memory chip, HBM5, and publicly introduced its HPB cooling solution for the first time. Chief Technology Officer Jae-Hyung Song confirmed that HPB technology has been validated on HBM4E chips and will be commercially deployed with HBM5.

Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, in his Computex 2026 keynote, said agentic AI is reshaping computing demand, and future AI infrastructure will enter a heterogeneous era of co-development among CPUs, GPUs, ASICs, and custom chips. Demand for CPUs in data centers is expected to re-enter a growth cycle.

SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won said the global shortage of memory chips may persist until 2030, and the company is increasing capital expenditures to bridge the supply-demand gap. SK Hynix plans to double its wafer capacity over the next five years.

ByteDance’s DouBao large model is expected to launch paid content in late June, with plans to integrate e-commerce functions in the third quarter to drive traffic to Douyin Mall. Longer-term operational strategies are not ruled out.

Anthropic announced the expansion of its Glasswing initiative to 150 new partner organizations across more than 15 countries. These institutions will use the Mythos tool to identify software vulnerabilities. New partners span energy, water, healthcare, telecommunications, and hardware industries.

OpenAI released a suite of new plugins for its Codex agent, covering public equity investment, banking, and sales use cases, and plans to introduce legal and corporate finance functions soon. OpenAI also intends to integrate Codex into ChatGPT.

Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen model launched Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal large model capable of understanding images and videos, performing deep reasoning, self-programming, tool invocation, validation, and autonomous iteration. It can complete complex, long-horizon tasks such as replicating smartphone apps and desktop software with a single command.

Global Macro & Policy

U.S. President Trump signed an executive order temporarily adjusting import tariffs on certain steel, aluminum, and copper products, effective June 8 through December 31, 2027. Import tariffs on harvesters and other agricultural machinery will be reduced from 25% to 15%, and the domestic content threshold for imported steel, aluminum, and copper will be lowered from 95% to 85%.

The United States plans to impose 25% punitive tariffs on a range of Brazilian imports, including electronic payment services, preferential tariff treatment, intellectual property protections, and ethanol market access. Exemptions include beef, coffee, rare earth minerals, aircraft and aircraft parts, crude oil, fertilizers, and numerous fruits and nuts.

The Canadian government has proposed to the U.S. and Mexico that the USMCA be renewed for 16 years to avoid triggering the agreement’s mandatory annual review process. Under USMCA provisions, the three nations are scheduled to conduct their first joint review in July. The agreement, signed in 2018 and effective since July 2020, is set to expire in 2036.

U.S. officials are reportedly discussing whether to deploy nuclear weapons in additional NATO European countries. The U.S. already deploys nuclear-capable bombers in Belgium, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Turkey, and the UK, and has signaled openness to additional deployments.

Hawkish voices within the Federal Reserve are intensifying. Cleveland Fed President Harker said while maintaining current rates remains appropriate, persistent inflation data may soon necessitate policy action — including potential further rate hikes — to counter elevated inflation risks.

Labor market resilience continues to bolster Fed hawkish expectations. U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data showed job openings rose to 7.62 million in April, the highest level in two years and well above the 6.87 million forecast. Layoffs fell to 1.692 million, significantly below expectations.

The European Union plans to impose strict eligibility criteria for cloud computing services in key public procurement projects, potentially excluding Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. The proposal aims to reduce EU dependence on U.S. tech products and promote domestic industry development.

Eurostat preliminary data showed euro area CPI rose 3.2% year-over-year in May, up from 3% in April, reinforcing market expectations of a modest ECB rate hike in June.

South Korea’s central bank is poised to raise rates. Statistics Korea reported that May CPI rose 3.1% year-over-year, the largest increase since March 2024. Core CPI increased 2.5%, the highest since February 2024.

The World Meteorological Organization released a report indicating most climate models predict this El Niño will develop into at least a moderate event and may intensify further. Seasonal forecasts suggest most regions will experience above-average temperatures in the coming months.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. major indices closed slightly higher: the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.45% to 51,307.79, the S&P 500 gained 0.13% to 7,609.78, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.03% to 27,093.90 — all closing at record highs. Cisco rose over 5%, Caterpillar gained more than 5%, leading the Dow. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index declined 1.05%, with Microsoft down over 4% and Google down nearly 4%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 1.83%, with Canadian Solar up over 12% and Hesai Group up over 8%. Traders closely monitored developments in U.S.-Iran relations and major tech stock performance.

European indices closed higher: Germany’s DAX rose 0.48% to 25,124.17, France’s CAC 40 gained 0.77% to 8,209.09, and the UK’s FTSE 100 climbed 0.33% to 10,373.51. Progress in ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the U.S. lifted risk appetite, but Brent crude breached $95 per barrel, maintaining upward pressure on inflation expectations.

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed. South Korea’s KOSPI swung sharply — initially surging before plunging more than 3% — and ultimately closed up 0.15% at 8,801.49, setting a new record. South Korea’s total market capitalization surpassed India’s, becoming the world’s sixth-largest stock market. Japan’s Nikkei 225 fell 0.3% to 66,734.24. India’s SENSEX rose 0.52% to 74,649.84.

Andrew Left, founder of the renowned short-selling firm Citron Research, was convicted by a U.S. court of stock manipulation and may face decades in prison. The case could mark a landmark moment for short-sellers globally and reshape regulatory boundaries for short reports and market commentary.

Data from India’s National Securities Depository Limited showed that as of June 1, foreign institutional investors’ net investment in Indian equities totaled 7.3 trillion rupees — the lowest since 2016. Global fund ownership in Indian equities has declined from nearly 20% a decade ago to 15% today.

Bonds

China’s interbank bond market traded in a narrow range, with yields on major government bonds moving slightly in different directions. Treasury futures rallied slightly at the close, with the 10-year contract up 0.06%. Liquidity conditions returned to ample levels as the PBOC conducted 200 million yuan in reverse repo operations, resulting in a net withdrawal of 248.8 billion yuan.

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed: the 2-year yield rose 1.24 basis points to 4.039%, the 3-year yield rose 0.59 basis points to 4.085%, the 5-year yield held steady at 4.163%, the 10-year yield fell 1.18 basis points to 4.439%, and the 30-year yield declined 1.21 basis points to 4.957%.

Commodities

U.S. crude oil futures rose 1.33% to $93.39 per barrel; Brent crude gained 1.01% to $95.94 per barrel. U.S. API crude inventories fell sharply, exceeding market expectations, while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East — including Iran’s suspension of U.S.-Iran talks and its plan to block the Strait of Hormuz — heightened concerns over supply disruptions. The strait handles roughly 20% of global oil shipments, and global crude inventories remain at low levels, jointly supporting higher prices.

International precious metals futures generally rose: COMEX gold futures gained 0.29% to $4,519.20 per ounce, and COMEX silver futures rose 0.25% to $75.44 per ounce. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East lifted safe-haven demand, while rising U.S. and European rate expectations weighed on market sentiment, creating a tug-of-war.

Most base metals on the LME rose: LME tin climbed 2.88% to $58,280 per ton, LME lead gained 1.52% to $2,037 per ton, LME zinc rose 1.48% to $3,631 per ton, LME aluminum advanced 1.20% to $3,760.50 per ton, LME copper increased 1.10% to $13,983.50 per ton, while LME nickel edged down 0.19% to $19,215 per ton.

The European Central Bank reported that as of the end of 2025, gold accounted for 27% of global official reserve assets, surpassing U.S. Treasuries, which fell to 22%. Gold has become the world’s largest official reserve asset.

Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) revealed plans to construct a refined products pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also exploring a new east-west crude oil trunk line that would allow other Gulf producers to route exports through UAE territory, avoiding the strait.

FX & Currency Markets

Onshore renminbi closed at 6.7622 against the U.S. dollar at 16:30, up 28 basis points from the previous close, and settled at 6.7650 in the night session. The PBOC’s mid-rate was set at 6.8187, a 20-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

At the close of New York trading, the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.03% to 99.22. Non-U.S. currencies were mixed: the euro fell 0.01% to 1.1631, the pound rose 0.07% to 1.3464, the Australian dollar rose 0.27% to 0.7180, the dollar rose 0.16% against the yen to 159.9295, the dollar fell 0.01% against the Canadian dollar to 1.3839, the dollar rose 0.08% against the Swiss franc to 0.7873, and the offshore renminbi gained 23 basis points to 6.7629.

Japan’s authorities issued a strong signal after record-sized foreign exchange interventions proved ineffective. Finance Minister Kato said the government stands ready to take appropriate action on exchange rates if necessary.

Original Air Date:  02.JUN.2026
8:00 PM EST

#1202 //  THE AI RACE - LIVE
In this first episode of The AI Race, we cut through the noise to deliver a clear-eyed survey of where artificial intelligence actually stands in 2026. We examine the real capabilities and hard limitations of current systems, the massive infrastructure and energy demands driving the buildout, and the technical paths — including photonics — that could help ease those pressures. We also explore the global competition shaping this technology, how narratives around AI and data centers are being shaped and amplified, and the serious questions of economic impact, power concentration, and governance that will determine who benefits. Along the way, we separate legitimate concerns from exaggerated fears while giving voice to the real experiences many had during recent years of information control. We close with late-breaking developments across geopolitics and global institutions, and a grounded look at what’s truly at stake as this race accelerates.
#QBits

https://t.co/uRVZ9Q4b5P

text:
the alpha corner is gold if you ask me 👀 (i'm obviously biased)

I built the daily brief because I was tired of reading the same AI take 40 times a day and still not knowing where the buildout was actually tightening.

So now every morning it goes through the whole bottleneck book, memory, packaging, photonics, power, grid, cooling, and flags what's coiled before it's obvious. great way to synthetise everything and find where the pressure is.

have a look for yourself!

text:
The AI infrastructure buildout is barely in its first innings.

Everyone on here is trying to figure out where in the stack they want to be.

Power. Compute. HPC DCs. Photonics. Memory. Networking. Cooling. Models/Apps.

Some of those layers I understand well. Some I understand just enough to be dangerous.

That is why I keep coming back to the most physical parts of the stack.

Land. Power. Buildings. Capacity. Hyperscalers and AI labs who need more compute than the world can currently provide.

That is $IREN to me.

The market spent years treating it like its old business, and probably still does to some extent until AI revenue really shows up.

I think the real story is power-backed infrastructure as compute becomes one of the scarcest resources on earth.

$DGXX is earlier, smaller and more execution heavy, but that is part of the appeal to me.

It feels closer to an early mini $NBIS style setup if they execute.

Same bet: compute demand is a 2 to 3 decade buildout.

The sheer deficit in compute gives me sleep at night comfort. That is why I have sized these names accordingly.

And dare I say it, I think this sector can 100x over the next few decades.

I think we are still early in figuring out who owns the real choke points.

People like @jiahanjimliu, @Agrippa_Inv and @FransBakker9812 are deeper in parts of this stack, but this is how I’m framing it.

Everyone on here is trying to figure out where in the stack they want to be. Power. Compute. HPC DCs. Photonics. Memory. Networking. Cooling. Models/Apps. Some of those layers I understand well. Some I understand just enough to be dangerous. That is why I keep coming back to the most physical parts of the stack. Land. Power. Buildings. Capacity. Hyperscalers and AI labs who need more compute than the world can currently provide. That is $IREN to me. The market spent years treating it like its old business, and probably still does to some extent until AI revenue really shows up. I think the real story is power-backed infrastructure as compute becomes one of the scarcest resources on earth. $DGXX is earlier, smaller and more execution heavy, but that is part of the appeal to me. It feels closer to an early mini $NBIS style setup if they execute. Same bet: compute demand is a 2 to 3 decade buildout. The sheer deficit in compute gives me sleep at night comfort. That is why I have sized these names accordingly. And dare I say it, I think this sector can 100x over the next few decades. I think we are still early in figuring out who owns the real choke points. People like @jiahanjimliu, @Agrippa_Inv and @FransBakker9812 are deeper in parts of this stack, but this is how I’m framing it."}}}, "rest_id": "2061949984417759713", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "13342", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
■「コンポーネント企業」のニッチな独占構造
＜高い参入障壁と認証の壁＞
宇宙空間は、激しい温度変化、放射線、真空状態、そして打ち上げ時の凄まじい振動に耐えなければなりません。
一度「宇宙での稼働実績（Flight Heritage）」を得たコンポーネントは、競合が容易にリプレイスできないため、先行者が市場を独占する構造です。

＜主要プレイヤー＞
Mynaric（$MYV / ドイツ・米国）:
LEO衛星向け光通信ターミナルのパイオニア。
SpaceX、DARPA、Northrop Grummanなどに製品を供給するセクターの代表格。

CACI International（$CACI）:
SA Photonicsを買収し、米政府・防衛向けの超高信頼性レーザー通信コンポーネントで圧倒的なシェア。

国内の動向:
ソニーグループが「ソニー宇宙光通信（SSC）」を設立して民間LEO方向への量産化を狙っているほか、三菱電機やNECなどの重電大手が防衛省向けに強みを持っています。

text:
Sivers Semiconductors Q1 是个很适合校准 CPO / optical interconnect 交易的例子。

公司 Q1 net sales 61.9 MSEK，同比 -22%；Photonics revenue 17.8 MSEK，同比 -32%。但同一份报告里，management 也说 opportunity pipeline YTD +77% 到 $799M，并继续强调 AI data center 的 800G+ pluggables、未来 CPO/NPO 机会。

这里最容易误判的是：方向对，不等于当季收入兑现；pipeline 很大，不等于 margin、cash flow、customer ramp 已经过关。

所以看 $CRDO $CIEN $LITE $COHR 这条线，我会把三件事分开：

1. 现实订单和出货；
2. gross margin / customer concentration；
3. CPO/NPO 真正商业化时间表。

CPO 是未来方向，但当季财报要先证明“现在能交付什么”。

text:
Daily Plan | Jun 1, 2026

Focus Themes
Software, Stablecoins, aluminum, rare earths.

Situational Awareness
Started to see a big pullback last week in the leaders: Space, Chip Testing, HPC Hosting, Photonics, Chip Power while money rotated into Software, Stablecoins, Edge/DN, Critical Minerals, Crytpo Miners and others.

Personally I believe that software is due for a run. I work in tech and I don't see software going away any time soon and I don't see vibe coding replacing the rigorous engineering work and skill needed to produce production level software, both internal and external. If anything, I expect iteration to increase and quality to improve. I think that vibe coding is real and more people will be able to build, so the overall pie will get bigger, but at the end of the day building and maintaining production software is a hard skill that companies will continue to pay for (both for the software itself and the talent to build it).

But I digress and the market doesn't care about my opinion! I've got my eyes on Software and Stabelcoins primarily, with a nod to Drones/Quantum for another pump. The Lithium trade as well seems to be gaining strength.

Market Commentary
QQQ and SPY eked out a few more gains but is that spinning top a evening star or just indecision before we head higher? I'm not the only voice echoing the need for a few weeks sideways but when you're in a bull market you have to just trade what you see.

Oil losing the 50 day, 10Y and UUP ticking down, productive macro backdrop. Major weakness in BTC and ETH. Gold and silver finding support. Copper trying to tighten up.

Personal Commentary
My goal for this week is patience and saying no to FOMO. I also need to spend more time on preparation, model book, and trade review. The past month has been challenging on the personal and professional life which didn't leave me as much time and energy for trading. Unfortunate during such a great run but I won't let that hold me back. The market is full of opportunities. I can hunt them down and trade them for great personal success.

Situational Awareness Started to see a big pullback last week in the leaders: Space, Chip Testing, HPC Hosting, Photonics, Chip Power while money rotated into Software, Stablecoins, Edge/DN, Critical Minerals, Crytpo Miners and others. Personally I believe that software is due for a run. I work in tech and I don't see software going away any time soon and I don't see vibe coding replacing the rigorous engineering work and skill needed to produce production level software, both internal and external. If anything, I expect iteration to increase and quality to improve. I think that vibe coding is real and more people will be able to build, so the overall pie will get bigger, but at the end of the day building and maintaining production software is a hard skill that companies will continue to pay for (both for the software itself and the talent to build it). But I digress and the market doesn't care about my opinion! I've got my eyes on Software and Stabelcoins primarily, with a nod to Drones/Quantum for another pump. The Lithium trade as well seems to be gaining strength. Market Commentary QQQ and SPY eked out a few more gains but is that spinning top a evening star or just indecision before we head higher? I'm not the only voice echoing the need for a few weeks sideways but when you're in a bull market you have to just trade what you see. Oil losing the 50 day, 10Y and UUP ticking down, productive macro backdrop. Major weakness in BTC and ETH. Gold and silver finding support. Copper trying to tighten up. Personal Commentary My goal for this week is patience and saying no to FOMO. I also need to spend more time on preparation, model book, and trade review. The past month has been challenging on the personal and professional life which didn't leave me as much time and energy for trading. 3. We need quantum cryogenic testing infrastructure -> $FORM 

4. Atomic level fabrication leaders -> $OXIG

Zero positions personally in any right now.

text:
Retail investors, listen carefully.

If you're aiming to grow your account to $1 million this year, there's one thing you need to understand.

How did we identify the storage sector before it took off, including:

Micron Technology ( $MU )
SanDisk ( $SNDK )

How did we spot the semiconductor rally before it exploded, including:

NVIDIA ( $NVDA )
Advanced Micro Devices ( $AMD )
Arm Holdings ( $ARM )

How did we identify the photonics opportunity before the sector gained momentum, including:

Applied Optoelectronics ( $AAOI )
Navitas Semiconductor ( $NVTS )
Astera Labs ( $ALAB )

And how did we find the software leaders before the sector moved higher, including:

ServiceNow ( $NOW )
Oracle ( $ORCL )
Salesforce ( $CRM )

The answer is actually very simple.

It wasn't because we had insider information.

It wasn't because we got lucky.

It's because we focus relentlessly on capital flows, industry trends, earnings growth, and market sentiment. We look for sectors and companies that institutions are quietly accumulating long before they become mainstream.

The biggest opportunities don't appear after they make headlines. They begin long before the crowd notices.

While most investors are focused on what went up yesterday, successful investors are researching what could lead tomorrow.

The key to investing isn't chasing momentum—it's identifying trends early.

It's not about predicting the market. It's about recognizing where money, fundamentals, and demand are headed before everyone else does.

Opportunities are always there. The question is whether you'll recognize them before the market catches on.

Every great stock you see today was once an overlooked name.

The real question is:

When the next opportunity emerges, will you spot it before the crowd does?

text:
With all this talk about connectivity $MRVL $AVGO $CRDO … 

I did a deep dive into the 1.6 T optical DSP… 

Ultimately the most underappreciated thing about $CRDO optical story isn’t the DSP. 

It’s that they now own nearly every block of a finished 1.6T module — SerDes, optical DSP (Bluebird/Cardinal), silicon photonics and laser drivers (DustPhotonics), the ZeroFlap transceiver assembly, and the PILOT reliability layer on top. 

$MRVL & $AVGO sell the brains — the DSP — into an ecosystem where a separate module maker sources lasers from one vendor and photonics from another and bolts it together. $CRDO is the single name on the whole thing.

Why that matters compounds four ways. 

1. ASP capture, and this one’s concrete: a discrete DSP or PIC carries two-digit ASPs, a complete ZeroFlap module carries three-digit ASPs. That’s why ZeroFlap is the single largest contributor to the >$600M FY27 optical guide despite being one of three lines. Integration converts straight into dollars per link.

2. co-design. When one team owns the DSP, photonics, and laser drivers, it tunes the interfaces together instead of designing to a spec and praying three vendors’ parts play nice. That’s where the “fewer lasers” architecture comes from — lower power, fewer failure points, less exposure to laser supply bottlenecks. A vendor stitching third-party components can’t optimize across boundaries it doesn’t control.

3. it sidesteps the fight Credo would lose. As a merchant DSP they’re the third entrant against Marvell (in volume) and Broadcom (entrenched), in a market where design wins are sticky because re-qualifying silicon is slow. Selling a whole module changes the customer’s question from “is Credo’s DSP better than Marvell’s” to “is this complete module better for my power and uptime constraints.” That’s a contest Credo can win on architecture instead of incumbency.

4. supply-chain control. With optical supply tight, owning more of the BOM in-house means fewer external dependencies gating shipments — and fewer lasers compounds it.

This is a focused-niche edge, not a moat against the giants’ scale. $CRDO wins where power and reliability matter more than raw volume — in-rack and scale-up fabric links. It doesn’t make them bigger or more manufacturing-deep than $MRVL or $AVGO, who can still out-supply them on commodity volume. 

The bet is owning a defensible, high-ASP slice — the module — rather than competing as one component among many. 

The >$600M optical guide and the ZeroFlap-led mix are the validation, not the spec sheet.

Not investment advice. I’m Long $CRDO.

text:
$AVGO KEY READ-THROUGHS FROM BROADCOM Q2 FY26 EARNINGS CALL

Broadcom’s Q2 FY26 call was one of the clearest confirmations to date that AI infrastructure demand is extending from a GPU-led procurement cycle into a broader, multi-year custom silicon, Ethernet networking, HBM, power, data-center financing, and private-cloud infrastructure cycle. The most important market signal was not simply that Broadcom’s AI semiconductor revenue reached $10.8B, up 143% YoY, but that Q2 AI semiconductor bookings exceeded $30B, Q3 AI semiconductor revenue was guided to $16B, FY26 AI semiconductor revenue was guided to $56B, and FY27 AI semiconductor revenue was reiterated at more than $100B. The call also extended demand visibility into 2028 and tied future growth to specific gigawatt-scale customer programs at Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and 2 additional customers. The broader read-through is highly constructive for the AI infrastructure supply chain, especially Ethernet networking, optical components, HBM, leading-edge foundry, advanced packaging, power equipment, data-center construction, and alternative asset managers financing AI compute. The principal negative implications are rising share pressure for merchant GPU/proprietary networking ecosystems, a tougher competitive setup for custom ASIC peers, higher capex and power-cost burdens for hyperscalers, and reduced support for the VMware-displacement narrative in enterprise infrastructure software.

AI ACCELERATORS, CUSTOM SILICON, AND CLOUD PLATFORMS

GOOGLE TPU ROADMAP APPEARS MATERIALLY DE-RISKED, SUPPORTING ALPHABET’S AI COST AND SUPPLY POSITION (READ-THROUGH 1)

Affected company: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, high magnitude.

Time horizon: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom stated that with Google it “entered into a long-term agreement to develop and supply multiple generations of TPUs and AI networking,” and that the relationship remains “strategic and very substantial.” Management also said Broadcom continues to deliver “vastly superior technology and execution compared to other alternatives” and that this “ensures that our business will sustain and grow for the foreseeable future.”

Transmission mechanism: Alphabet’s AI compute roadmap appears less dependent on merchant GPUs than investor debate often assumes. Broadcom’s comments imply strong continuity in Google’s TPU roadmap across multiple generations, with Broadcom supplying both custom accelerator silicon and AI networking. This supports Google Cloud, Gemini, Vertex AI, and internal AI workloads by lowering cost per token, reducing external GPU supply risk, and preserving architectural control over model training and inference infrastructure. The read-through is especially important because Google can monetize TPUs internally and externally through cloud services, while also avoiding some of the margin leakage associated with buying large quantities of merchant accelerators at scarcity pricing.

The near-term trading catalyst is validation that Google has secured a long-duration TPU supply path during an industry-wide capacity squeeze. The longer-duration shift is that Alphabet’s custom silicon platform may become a more durable strategic differentiator as AI workloads scale into 2027 and 2028. The key offset is that Broadcom acknowledged Google will maintain source diversity given the scale of its AI compute demand, so the read-through is not exclusivity; it is strong roadmap validation and reduced execution risk.

CUSTOM XPU ADOPTION BY FRONTIER LABS CREATES LONGER-DURATION SHARE PRESSURE FOR MERCHANT GPU ECOSYSTEMS (READ-THROUGH 2)

Affected companies: NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA: US), Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Negative, medium magnitude for NVIDIA and AMD over the longer duration; limited near-term negative because total AI compute demand is still expanding rapidly.

Time horizon: Longer-duration fundamental shift, with intermittent trading sensitivity around hyperscaler capex and custom silicon news flow.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom guided FY26 AI semiconductor revenue to $56B, up approximately 180% from FY25, and reiterated FY27 AI semiconductor revenue “in excess of $100 billion.” Management said, “Demand for XPUs and networking is simply insatiable,” disclosed more than $30B of AI semiconductor bookings in Q2 against $10.8B shipped, and laid out gigawatt-scale custom XPU deployments for Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and 2 other customers.

Transmission mechanism: The call supports the view that custom accelerators are no longer isolated internal hyperscaler projects; they are becoming a central infrastructure layer for the largest AI model developers and platforms. This is structurally negative for the long-duration revenue share opportunity of merchant GPUs, especially in large, predictable, hyperscale training and inference clusters where customers can justify custom silicon development. For NVIDIA, the risk is not near-term unit weakness, as industry AI compute demand remains supply-constrained and expanding, but rather that the incremental dollar share of frontier AI capex gradually shifts toward customer-specific XPUs and Ethernet-based cluster architectures. For AMD, the read-through is similar but smaller in absolute magnitude because AMD’s accelerator share is lower and its AI opportunity is still more nascent.

The call also has a networking-specific negative implication for NVIDIA’s proprietary networking moat. Broadcom emphasized Ethernet scale-up, scale-out, PCIe switching, DSPs, lasers, and Jericho fabrics as critical to large XPU and GPU clusters. If Ethernet becomes the default AI networking architecture across more custom XPU deployments, NVIDIA’s InfiniBand-led system advantage could face greater pressure over time. The negative read-through should be framed as share-of-incremental-spend risk, not an immediate demand cliff.

BROADCOM’S DISCLOSED CUSTOMER MOMENTUM RAISES RELATIVE COMPETITIVE RISK FOR CUSTOM ASIC AND OPTICAL DSP PEERS (READ-THROUGH 3)

Affected company: Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Net negative, medium magnitude on relative positioning; positive for total addressable market but negative for perceived share capture.

Time horizon: Near-term trading risk and longer-duration competitive concern.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom disclosed 6 core AI customers, more than $30B of Q2 AI bookings, FY26 AI semiconductor guidance of $56B, and FY27 AI semiconductor guidance of more than $100B. Management also stated that in networking Broadcom has “at least one generation of technology and product leadership,” that its 100Tb Ethernet switch has been shipping for more than 1 year, that a 200Tb switch will tape out this quarter, and that in CPO, 1.6Tb DSPs, CW lasers, and EML lasers, Broadcom is “the de-facto standard in the industry.”

Transmission mechanism: The total custom ASIC and AI networking TAM expansion is positive for all credible suppliers, including Marvell. However, the specific call disclosures reinforce Broadcom’s dominant position in the highest-value custom XPU and AI networking programs. The risk for Marvell is that investors extrapolate Broadcom’s 6-customer roadmap, Google agreement, OpenAI production timing, Anthropic gigawatt access, Meta MTIA partnership, and 1.6Tb optical/networking claims into a larger Broadcom share of the custom AI silicon profit pool than previously assumed.

The near-term catalyst is relative stock pressure when investors compare Broadcom’s disclosed bookings, customer specificity, and 2027 revenue guide against Marvell’s own AI revenue trajectory and customer disclosures. The longer-duration concern is that Broadcom’s bundled capability across XPU design, Ethernet switching, SerDes, DSPs, lasers, PCIe, and routing creates a higher barrier to displacement than a single-chip custom silicon engagement would imply. The read-through is not that Marvell cannot grow in AI; it is that Broadcom’s call increased the evidentiary burden for Marvell’s share-gain narrative.

META’S MTIA ROADMAP IS VALIDATED, BUT THE REVENUE AND EFFICIENCY BENEFITS ARE LARGELY 2027-2028 EVENTS (READ-THROUGH 4)

Affected company: Meta Platforms, Inc. (META: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, medium magnitude longer term; neutral to modestly negative near term from capex intensity.

Time horizon: Longer-duration fundamental shift, limited near-term earnings impact.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom stated that for Meta it announced a partnership to deliver multiple generations of MTIA XPUs and expects to deploy 3GW through the end of 2028. Management added that the initial 1GW order, including XPUs and networking, has been received and will start delivery in 2H27.

Transmission mechanism: Meta’s custom accelerator roadmap is becoming more concrete. The Broadcom call supports the view that MTIA is transitioning from an internal strategic ambition into a multi-generation, multi-gigawatt deployment plan. This is positive for Meta’s long-term AI infrastructure economics because successful custom inference and training silicon can lower cost per recommendation, cost per generated token, and dependence on external accelerator supply. It also improves Meta’s ability to optimize AI hardware around its own ranking, ads, recommendations, generative AI, and Llama workloads.

The near-term implication is more muted because deliveries start in 2H27, meaning capex planning, infrastructure buildout, and engineering spend occur before material efficiency benefits are visible in earnings. The longer-duration implication is more constructive: if Meta can scale MTIA across 3GW through 2028, internal silicon becomes a meaningful lever against AI margin dilution.

ETHERNET AI NETWORKING AND SYSTEMS

Affected companies: Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET: US), Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, high magnitude for Arista; positive, medium magnitude for Cisco.

Time horizon: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom stated that networking represented almost 40% of Q2 AI revenue. Management said, “networking is key to building scalable XPU and GPU clusters,” and highlighted leadership in 200G and 400G SerDes, Ethernet and PCI Express switches, the industry’s only 100Tb Ethernet switch shipping for more than 1 year, a 200Tb switch taping out this quarter, and Jericho 3 and Jericho 4 fabric solutions for large multi-hyperscaler deployments.

Transmission mechanism: The call is a clear positive for Ethernet-based AI networking. Broadcom’s silicon roadmap and AI networking revenue mix imply that large-scale AI clusters are increasingly being designed around Ethernet fabrics rather than only proprietary networking alternatives. This expands Arista’s addressable opportunity in AI back-end networks, front-end networks, and inter-data-center fabrics. Cisco benefits as well, although with a more diversified and less hyperscale-pure exposure profile.

The longer-duration shift is that Ethernet appears to be gaining credibility as a default scale-out and scale-across architecture for both XPUs and GPUs. The key nuance is that Broadcom expects networking to normalize closer to 30% of AI revenue over time as XPU revenue ramps, so the positive read-through is stronger for absolute networking dollar growth than for sustained mix expansion.

OPTICAL COMPONENTS AND 1.6T ECOSYSTEM DEMAND GET ANOTHER STRONG CONFIRMATION (READ-THROUGH 6)

Affected companies: Coherent Corp. (COHR: US), Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE: US), Fabrinet (FN: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, medium-to-high magnitude.

Time horizon: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom stated that in “CPOs, which is Co-Packaged Optics, 1.6 terabit DSPs, CW and EML lasers, we are the de-facto standard in the industry.” Management also emphasized AI networking demand, Jericho 3 and Jericho 4 fabric solutions, and a next-generation 200Tb switch tapeout this quarter.

Transmission mechanism: AI cluster scaling requires materially more optical bandwidth, higher-speed interconnects, and a transition toward 1.6T-class optical architectures. Coherent and Lumentum benefit through lasers, optical components, and high-speed photonics exposure. Fabrinet benefits through optical transceiver and photonics manufacturing leverage. Broadcom’s comments indicate that the transition to higher-speed optics is not merely a forward-looking technology roadmap; it is being pulled by real AI cluster deployment requirements.

The near-term trading catalyst is confirmation that hyperscale AI networking demand is supporting a rapid move toward 1.6T optical architectures. The longer-duration shift is that optical content per AI data center should continue rising as clusters scale across racks, halls, and data centers. The principal offset is that Broadcom’s own DSP, CPO, and laser capabilities may capture a larger portion of the value chain, limiting the breadth of benefits for standalone optical component vendors if vertical integration accelerates.

SEMICONDUCTOR SUPPLY CHAIN, MEMORY, FOUNDRY, PACKAGING, AND TEST

HBM DEMAND HAS MULTI-YEAR VISIBILITY, WITH POSITIVE READ-THROUGH FOR LEADING MEMORY SUPPLIERS (READ-THROUGH 7)

Affected companies: SK hynix Inc. (000660: South Korea), Micron Technology, Inc. (MU: US), Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (005930: South Korea)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, high magnitude for SK hynix; positive, medium-to-high magnitude for Micron and Samsung.

Time horizon: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom stated that it has secured supply for 2026 and 2027 and is working on 2028 and 2029. In response to a supply question referencing wafers and HBM, management said customers have continued to ask for incremental supply and that “by and large” Broadcom has been able to support it. Later in the call, management said XPU content per gigawatt will increase as chips become multi-die and incorporate “lots of HBM.”

Transmission mechanism: Broadcom’s custom XPU ramp is HBM-intensive. FY26 AI semiconductor revenue guidance of $56B, FY27 guidance of more than $100B, and 2028 visibility imply substantial multi-year HBM pull-through. SK hynix is the most direct positive read-through given its leadership in high-bandwidth memory, while Micron and Samsung benefit from incremental industry demand, customer diversification, and the need for multiple qualified suppliers.

The near-term catalyst is the combination of $30B-plus Q2 AI semiconductor bookings and management commentary that customers are requesting incremental supply. The longer-duration shift is that HBM demand is becoming tied not only to merchant GPU platforms but also to a broader set of custom XPUs across Google, Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and other customers. This broadens the demand base for HBM and supports a tighter pricing and allocation environment.

LEADING-EDGE FOUNDRY, ADVANCED PACKAGING, AND AI TEST DEMAND ARE STRUCTURALLY TIGHTER (READ-THROUGH 8)

Affected companies: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (2330: Taiwan; TSM: US ADR), ASE Technology Holding Co. (3711: Taiwan), BE Semiconductor Industries N.V. (BESI: Netherlands), Advantest Corp. (6857: Japan), Teradyne, Inc. (TER: US), ASML Holding N.V. (ASML: Netherlands), Applied Materials, Inc. (AMAT: US)

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive, high magnitude for TSMC; positive, medium magnitude for advanced packaging, test, and equipment beneficiaries.

Time horizon: Near-term trading catalyst for capacity-tightness narratives; longer-duration fundamental shift for capex and utilization.

Supporting call commentary/data point: Broadcom disclosed more than $30B of Q2 AI semiconductor bookings, guided FY26 AI semiconductor revenue to $56B, reiterated more than $100B in FY27 AI semiconductor revenue, and said supply has been secured for 2026 and 2027 while 2028 and 2029 planning is underway. Management also said customers are requesting incremental supply and that XPU content per gigawatt will rise as chips become more complex, multi-die, and HBM-rich.

Transmission mechanism: Broadcom is fabless, so its AI semiconductor growth converts into external demand for leading-edge wafer starts, advanced packaging, substrate capacity, HBM integration, and high-complexity test. TSMC is the clearest beneficiary because custom AI accelerators and high-performance networking silicon are highly likely to consume leading-edge foundry and advanced packaging capacity. ASE, BE Semiconductor, Advantest, and Teradyne benefit from the rising complexity of multi-die AI packages, higher test intensity, and packaging process demand. ASML and Applied Materials benefit more indirectly as sustained AI demand supports foundry and packaging capex.

The near-term catalyst is evidence that Broadcom’s customers are ordering ahead into 2028, reinforcing tightness in advanced supply chains. The longer-duration shift is that custom AI accelerators are becoming a second major demand pillar alongside merchant GPUs for leading-edge foundry and advanced packaging capacity.

Management also said customers are requesting incremental supply and that XPU content per gigawatt will rise as chips become more complex, multi-die, and HBM-rich.

Transmission mechanism: Broadcom is fabless, so its AI semiconductor growth converts into external demand for leading-edge wafer starts, advanced packaging, substrate capacity, HBM integration, and high-complexity test. TSMC is the clearest beneficiary because custom AI accelerators and high-performance networking silicon are highly likely to consume leading-edge foundry and advanced packaging capacity. ASE, BE Semiconductor, Advantest, and Teradyne benefit from the rising complexity of multi-die AI packages, higher test intensity, and packaging process demand. ASML and Applied Materials benefit more indirectly as sustained AI demand supports foundry and packaging capex.

The near-term catalyst is evidence that Broadcom’s customers are ordering ahead into 2028, reinforcing tightness in advanced supply chains.
