text:
Decided to size up a bit on my #1 favourite stock last week.

After enjoying the gains in photonics and memory earlier this year with $SOI (entered at €48), $AAOI (entered at $47) and $MU (entered at $338), I've moved on to just $EOS.AX. ($GP6 is the European ticker of $EOS.AX, for diversification purposes)

EOS is becoming a full stack air defense prime. They offer sensors, AI command and control (MARSS NiDAR), kinetic effectors (RWS, interceptor drones, rocket and missile systems), and directed-energy lasers (Apollo 100 kW today, 300 kW C-RAM in development). The kill chain is combat proven in Ukraine and Iran, the order book is at an inflection point. The market still treats it like a niche Australian small cap rather than the global defense prime it's turning into.

AI / Photonics / Memory is starting to feel a little hot after a huge run, where the reward doesn't justify the risk for me personally. Defense stocks are still beaten down, even after their jump following the Trump investment news. 

Especially C-UAS focused stocks, after the Iran conflict exposed how unprepared the West still is, are structurally underappreciated. The US has spent billions on million-dollar Patriot and SM-2 interceptors trying to shoot down $20-50K Shahed drones, a cost equation that simply cannot scale. 

The Australian market has lagged the S&P 500 too, and if a deal with Iran is actually close it should benefit Australia a lot. I also believe that the next 6-12 months should bring the contract conversions that force a rerate in the stock: massive Apollo lasers, MARSS and RWS orders worth multiple billions of dollars. I expect Europe and the Middle East to be major customers in the near future.

text:
$LITE - LUMENTUM GAVE BACK 20% FROM ITS PEAK ON ZERO COMPANY NEWS — THE OPTICS TRADE IS JUST DIGESTING

LITE fell ~10% today and is now down more than 20% from its recent high, but there's no company-specific catalyst — it's a sector-wide pullback across photonics names after a parabolic AI-optical run. Analysts still carry a Buy consensus, and demand for optical interconnects in AI data centers keeps building.

The structural case is the co-design problem: at hyperscale, moving a bit over copper now costs more energy than the compute it carries, so the interconnect flips to light. That's why co-packaged optics and external laser sources are the next bottleneck — and Lumentum sits on the laser and transceiver supply side of exactly that shift. The pullback is positioning unwinding, not a thesis break.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
Nokia is bolting Nvidia GPUs directly into its cell towers, and the reason is a number: 200 milliseconds.

A factory robot can't wait that long for a cloud server to think. So the thinking moves to the tower.

This is the edge-computing thesis finally getting concrete. Instead of routing every AI decision to a distant data center and back, $NOK turns base stations into tiny compute nodes, running the inference where the signal already is. T-Mobile was the first US carrier to pilot it. Nvidia's CEO described a future where every radio network becomes an AI network.

The latency you can't engineer away is the latency of distance.

Underneath the headline, Nokia quietly rebuilt itself. Its $2.3 billion purchase of Infinera turned it from a reseller of someone else's photonics into a vertically integrated optical company with its own chips.

Optical networks grew 20% year over year last quarter. Management raised infrastructure guidance into the teens.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

text:
❗ONLY 9 Hours Left❗

[Last Call for Substack/Full article]

Pricing update — effective June 3, 2026 (11:59PM Pacific Time)

PhotonCap started with a simple bet: that there's demand for silicon photonics research that goes deeper than press releases and earnings recaps.

That bet has paid off.

What PhotonCap does differently:

This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another.

Here's what that looks like in practice:

Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value.

Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports.

Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level.

PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean.

From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately.

Where we stand — and where we're going:

PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members.

Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider.

New pricing — effective June 3 (11:59 PM, Pacific Time):

Monthly: $50/month (currently $30)

Annual: $500/year (currently $300)

Already a paid subscriber? Your rate is locked in permanently. Nothing changes for you.

If you've been considering subscribing, now is the time. Lock in the current rate before June 3.

Content offerings, format, and frequency may evolve over time as PhotonCap grows.

— PhotonCap

When the energy foundation of a formation becomes this volatile, it signals the formation is no longer self-sustaining. The thermal combustion era is not ending slowly — it is convulsing.
Third, Democracy-as-productive-force under geopolitical stress. The US-Iran war is the Democracy column acting out its terminal contradiction: military power deployed to defend the thermal supply chain, while the same democracy-capital system is funding the AI/compute transition that makes that thermal supply chain obsolete. JPMorgan argues that accelerating oil inventory depletion will ultimately force the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz — possibly in June  (CNBC) . Even resolution confirms the diagnosis: the old formation can only survive by emergency management of its own energy base.
FARWATERS SIGNALS
In the context of the matrix, the following moves are formational signals, not just price action:
Marvell at +32% on Nvidia photonics partnership is the advanced packaging / optical interconnect farwater activating. This maps directly to Hanmi Semiconductor's territory: CoWoS, advanced packaging, interconnect density — the hardware layer of the AI productive force.
Navitas Semiconductor soaring on a partnership with Nvidia, with its 800V-to-6V DC-DC power delivery board displayed at the Nvidia showcase  (CNBC) — this is the power management / new energy efficiency farwater. Navitas operates in the same column as VINA TECH supercapacitors: making compute energy-efficient is how the new formation solves its own energy equation without thermal combustion dependency.
Coherent getting Nvidia backing for AI datacenter optics expansion is the compute-efficiency signal. Leeno Industrial and Openedges Technology's logic — test sockets for advanced chips, AI IP for efficiency — follows the same directional arrow.
RISKS BY THE MATRIX
The thermal supply shock risk remains the single largest formational risk. The World Bank projects Brent averaging $95–$115 in 2026 under current disruption scenarios — 10 to 35 percent above baseline  (World Bank) . This is a direct tax on the old formational capital base and a potential trigger for capital reversal from growth assets if the shock deepens unexpectedly.
Geopolitical containment risk is secondary: any escalation that draws China into the Hormuz equation — as the largest buyer of Gulf crude — creates a multi-formation crisis that no single policy instrument can contain.
AI capex reversal risk is the new formation's own vulnerability. Nvidia has declined in 7 of the last 10 sessions  (https://t.co/tF181fPTDi) , signaling that even the new formational flagship is subject to capital rotation when macro fear spikes. NVDA ex-dividend today (June 4, $0.25) is a minor technical event, but the pattern of high-volume selling into strength warns that Capitalist subjects are still booking profits in the transition zone — not yet fully committed to the new paradigm.
VERDICT: ACCELERATION SCORE — HIGH
Today's configuration — thermal combustion convulsing at $96 oil, embodied intelligence infrastructure being built at record speed by Nvidia/Marvell/Navitas, and capital visibly migrating between formations — represents one of the highest-velocity transition moments the matrix has recorded.
The farwaters are not waiting for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen. The new formational productive forces are being built because the old energy base is unstable. That is precisely the dynamic Pulatov's matrix predicts: the new formation does not replace the old at peak stability — it emerges through the crisis of the old.

text:
$ABNB (Airbnb) and $ALMU (Aeluma) delivered standout performances today.
$ABNB: Driven by new summer features and the 2026 World Cup, Q1 revenue rose 18% and guidance was raised; a leader in the travel recovery.
ALMU: Breakthrough in 300mm photonics platform and accelerated government quantum contracts; an early-stage commercialization play in AI and quantum computing.
Advantages: Combines consumer recovery with cutting-edge technology; low valuation with significant growth potential.
Should we continue monitoring: Yes, with multiple near-term catalysts and substantial mid-to-long-term potential.

#NVDA #AAPL #ABNB #ALMU #MU #TSLA #AMZN

In addition to the smart ring, new ATMs and payment terminals, and a live stand, Sber presented several other new developments at the SPIEF.

One of these is GigaNetwork. This is a platform where AI agents autonomously perform routine business processes. They search for counterparties, conduct tenders, and support transactions at all stages.

An optical computer for AI tasks was also presented. This is a new type of device: it uses light, not electronics, for calculations. According to the developers, the transition to photonics will reduce the costs of training and using large models. This is a real way to build an efficient computing infrastructure.

text:
Is it weird to say I still feel bullish despite the massive collapse today? 6/9 stocks in my port are down over 10% today, with SK hynix taking the questionable throne, dropping a staggering 18.6%. This is the worst day for my port in 2026 so far.

Despite that, I still feel bullish, because $META is reportedly planning an equity issuance worth $10's of billions of dollars (per FT), following $GOOG's equity issuance earlier this week. This would mean even more CapEx spending, thus even more spending on memory and photonics. 

That is bullish for $MU, $SNDK, SK hynix and $AAOI in my port. For now, the charts look extremely ugly. Longer term, I remain bullish.

text:
$LITE $COHR $AAOI 

I’m obviously a huge Nvidia bull.

I’m a huge quantum bull.

I’m a huge memory bull.

And I’m a huge physical AI / Tesla bull.

Having said that, there is one category that stands out as potentially offering the best generational buying opportunity b/t now and the end of this correction. It is AI networking / optical (photonics) interconnects.

This category is poised to face a tighter supply bottleneck than chips and even memory during the next phase of AI infrastructure buildout. Personally, I’m already building my photonics portfolio w/ a 2 year holding horizon. Note that this is distinct from my quantum positions which I plan to hold for many more years.

text:
🚨 CONFIRMED via Finansinspektionen (Swedish FSA): JPMorgan Chase & Co. just crossed the 5% ownership threshold in $SIVE.

Official filing:
→ Holder: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
→ Transaction date: June 2, 2026 (same day as the GlobalFoundries announcement)
→ Reason for flagging: "Köp" (BUY)
→ Pre-transaction position: 0 shares
→ Post-transaction position: 16,098,583 shares / 5.25008% of total voting rights

Position structure:
→ J.P. Morgan Securities plc (UK entity): 15,916,852 voting rights — 5.19%
→ J.P. Morgan Securities LLC (US entity): 181,731 voting rights — 0.06%
→ Direct shares: 10,049,558 (3.14% of total shares)
→ Cash-settled instruments (likely swaps): 6,049,025 share equivalents

Why this is structurally significant:

Swedish law requires mandatory disclosure when crossing the 5% ownership threshold. JPMorgan went from ZERO to 5.25% on June 2. The flagging reason filed with the FSA was explicitly "Köp" (Buy) — not custody, not market-making inventory. This is a deliberate institutional position.

The timing is what makes it remarkable:

June 2 was the same day GlobalFoundries announced the silicon photonics reference design partnership and $SIVE moved +50-70%.

Three possible reads:
JPM was positioned beforehand and captured the move

JPM built the position aggressively into and after the news.

JPM is positioning ahead of the Nasdaq NY listing in an underwriter / placement agent role

All three are bullish. The third would be the most strategically significant — tier-1 US investment banks don't take 5% positions in Swedish micro-caps without strategic context.

Connecting back to the "zero institutional ownership" post earlier this week:

Five days ago: $SIVE had zero US institutional 13F filings.

Today: the world's largest investment bank discloses a 5%+ position triggered by a "Buy" transaction.

The institutional layer that was missing has started forming.
The composition tells you it's sophisticated:
6 million of the 16 million share-equivalents are in cash-settled derivatives. The rest is direct shares. This is the kind of structured position you set up when you're a major capital markets player preparing for a multi-event sequence — index inclusions, dual listing, sector rotation.

JPMorgan doesn't take 5% positions in $2-3B Swedish micro-caps because they're bored.

$SIVE just got its first major Wall Street institutional anchor.

$SIVE

text:
5/6
Hybrid quantum classical systems are where the magic happens. No single platform wins alone. We’re seeing:
• Neutral atoms for scalable logic
• Photonics for networking & sensing
• Cryo-CMOS + magnons for compact control/memory
• AI agents running microsecond corrections (like in fusion)  This multi modal stack is accelerating the roadmap toward real advantage in materials, optimization, chemistry, and beyond years faster than single tech predictions.

text:
Quantum computing just went multi-platform and it’s mind blowing.  2026 isn’t about one winner taking all. Google just announced it’s adding neutral-atom systems alongside its superconducting roadmap. Companies are mixing neutral atoms, photonics, diamond spins, and more creating hybrid powerhouses.  
The era of “my qubits vs yours” is over. It’s now about smart combinations that actually deliver useful advantage. Let’s unpack why this feels like the real tipping point.

text:
Every dollar of TSMC's projected $500 billion chip-market upgrade depends on a technology the industry doesn't actually have yet at scale. The wiring inside today's AI chips physically can't carry tomorrow's workloads. Photonics is the only exit.

$TSM

text:
Aggregate inference compute demand grows regardless of where inference runs (Jevons paradox), which is the core of the bull case for hardware. 

Inference is structurally more open than training: Less CUDA-locked, more price sensitive. Regardless, I think enterprise will forever rely on neoclouds to reduce hardware CAPEX + in-house teams, so overarching investment thesis (whether big AI or locally hosted) is still the same. 

Stacked silicon, Photonics, Power Grids etc as y'all have been doing DD.

text:
$STM at a new all-time high after hours. 🥂

The craziest part is this hasn’t hit the mainstream yet. 

STM is quietly ramping high-volume production of its PIC100 silicon photonics platform (800G and 1.6T optical transceivers) for hyperscaler AI clusters, with plans to quadruple capacity by 2027￼ backed by long-term customer reservations. 

The anchor underneath it all is a multi-year, multi-billion dollar AWS engagement ￼spanning connectivity, microcontrollers, and power ICs, which even came with warrants to AWS for up to 24.8 million ST shares ￼ that vest as AWS buys product.

Key Takeaway:
Today’s after-hours action was dominated by micro-cap and small-cap names with very low floats, leading to exaggerated percentage moves. $XOS stands out with an incredible 132% surge — likely driven by major news or contract announcement.  These kinds of moves are high-risk, high-reward and often reverse quickly at the open. Always check volume, catalysts, and news before jumping in.  Trading Advice:  Use limit orders and tight risk management  
Many of these names have extremely low liquidity  
Watch for continuation or reversal in pre-market tomorrow

❌ طلبية J&J صغيرة نسبياً (250 ألف دولار فقط)

▪️نشاطها : 
- أنظمة الليزر الصناعية
- إزالة الصدأ والتنظيف بالليزر
- أنظمة دفاعية مضادة للدرونز (LSAD)
- تطبيقات طبية عبر شركة CMS Laser التابعة لها

▪️الخلاصة : 

السوق لا يسعر ما حدث اليوم فقط…
بل احتمال أن تتحول شركة صغيرة قيمتها 80 مليون دولار إلى شركة دفاعية تمتلك عقوداً حكومية مستقبلاً

ملاحظة : السهم غير شرعي ، فقط مشاركة معلومات للسهم

水曜朝ニュース（6/3 JST）

AIデータセンター、GPUだけ見てると次を取り逃がしそう。

$NVDA はVera Rubinで tokens per MW を前面に。$MRVL は「次のボトルネックはconnectivity」と整理。NVIDIAもSpectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsで電力効率5倍を打ち出した。

TrendForceは2027年HBM4契約価格の大幅上昇観測。$MU には追い風。

次は光I/O、HBM、電力・冷却、先進パッケージング。$LITE $COHR $VRT $TSM も見る。

text:
마벨(Marvell) CEO, "구리의 벽(Copper Wall)이 랙 내부로 이동하고 있으며, 이를 해결할 유일한 방법은 공동 패키징 광학(CPO)뿐"이라고 밝혀

*마벨 테크놀로지(Marvell Technology)의 CEO 맷 머피(Matt Murphy)는 "AI 인프라의 다음 주요 병목 현상은 연산(Compute)이나 메모리가 아니라, 바로 연결성(Connectivity)

*머피 CEO는 구리(Copper)에서 광학(Optical) 인터커넥트로의 전환이 먼 미래가 아닌 '지금 당장' 일어나고 있으며, 이는 반도체 산업 역사상 전례가 없을 정도로 거대한 수요의 물결을 일으킬 것이라고 말했음.

*구리의 벽(Copper Wall)의 물리학

- 신호가 구리 케이블을 통해 이동할 수 있는 거리는 대역폭에 반비례

- 레인당 100Gbps 수준: 구리 케이블은 약 5미터까지 도달할 수 있어 하나의 랙(Rack) 내부에서 서버들을 연결하기에 충분.

- ​레인당 200Gbps 수준: 그 거리는 약 2.5미터로 줄어들며, 이는 랙의 높이와 내부 라우팅 요구 사항을 감안할 때 딱 한계치에 해당.

- ​레인당 400Gbps 수준: 현재 업계가 나아가고 있는 이 단계에서는 구리선으로 랙 내부를 완전히 연결하는 것이 더 이상 불가능.

*구리의 벽이 한 단계 이동할 때마다, 광학으로 전환해야 하는 연결의 수는 최소 10배 이상 증가. 머피 CEO는 이를 광학 산업의 '수요 폭발'로 묘사.

*​공동 패키징 광학(CPO): 왜 필요한가?

- 랙 내부에서의 전환은 랙과 랙 사이(Inter-rack)에 사용되는 기존의 광학 기술로는 해결할 수 없다. 랙 내부의 연결 수는 랙 간 연결 수보다 약 10배 더 많음. 이 정도의 밀도에서 표준 광학 모듈을 장착하는 것은 전력과 공간 부족으로 인해 물리적으로 불가능.

- ​CPO는 광 엔진(Optical Engines)을 스위치나 연산 패키지에 직접 통합함으로써 이 문제를 해결. 광섬유를 전면 패널이 아닌 칩(Chip)으로 직접 가져오는 방식. 이 기술은 최첨단 CMOS 공정, 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh), 첨단 패키징, 그리고 광 인터커넥트를 하나의 긴밀하게 통합된 시스템으로 결합.

- 엔비디아(Nvidia)의 베라 루빈(Vera Rubin) 플랫폼이 이미 생산 단계의 첫 CPO 기반 스위치인 '스펙트럼-X 이더넷 포토닉스(Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics)'를 도입했다는 점을 언급하며, 이러한 전환이 더 이상 이론에 머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시.

*오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨.

*​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨.

*머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조.

text:
AI depends on physical infrastructure: chips, photonics, optical interconnects and data centers. Our new article explores where amorphous fluoropolymers may fit in the AI value chain.

https://t.co/qMKbgEzsSR

#AI #Photonics #Materials

Vad jag vet har Sivers aldrig haft något PM med GF inom Photonics. Så egentligen är det första gången det blir officiellt. Men så klar,  kunde man haft med en notis och att man varit ecosystem partners på olika sätt. Dock är det nog nedan OCI MSA partners (som inte heller nämn), som egentligen gör nyheten extra intressant.

text:
🚀 8 ETF Đang Xây Dựng Tương Lai Của AI

AI không chỉ là NVIDIA hay ChatGPT. Đằng sau là cả một hệ sinh thái gồm chip, bộ nhớ, điện năng, quang tử, robot và không gian.

💡 FOTO — Quang tử & truyền dữ liệu AI
“Hệ thần kinh” của AI với laser, silicon photonics và hạ tầng kết nối tốc độ cao.

🧠 DRAM — Bộ nhớ AI
Đặt cược vào HBM, NAND và các công ty lưu trữ dữ liệu cho kỷ nguyên AI.

⚛️ NLR — Năng lượng hạt nhân
Nguồn điện dài hạn cho các trung tâm dữ liệu và siêu máy tính AI.

🚀 NASA — Kinh tế không gian
Vệ tinh, tên lửa và hạ tầng cho nền kinh tế ngoài Trái Đất.

🤖 HUMN — Robot & AI hiện thân
Đầu tư vào robot hình người và tự động hóa thế hệ mới.

🔬 EUV — Thiết bị chế tạo chip
Những cỗ máy tạo ra các con chip AI tiên tiến nhất thế giới.

💻 SMH — Bán dẫn toàn cầu
Tiếp cận toàn bộ chuỗi giá trị ngành chip.

⚡ GRID — Hạ tầng điện
Các công ty nâng cấp lưới điện để đáp ứng nhu cầu năng lượng bùng nổ.

🎯 Nếu AI là một cơ thể:
🧠 DRAM = Trí nhớ
💻 SMH = Bộ não
🔬 EUV = Nhà máy sản xuất chip
💡 FOTO = Hệ thần kinh
⚡ GRID = Hệ tuần hoàn năng lượng
⚛️ NLR = Nguồn điện
🤖 HUMN = Cơ thể robot
🚀 NASA = Không gian mở rộng tương lai

Đây là 8 chủ đề đầu tư đang nằm ở trung tâm của siêu chu kỳ AI trong thập kỷ tới.

💡 $FOTO CHÍNH THỨC NIÊM YẾT – ETF ĐẦU TIÊN TẬP TRUNG VÀO TOÀN BỘ CHUỖI GIÁ TRỊ QUANG TỬ

Nếu chip là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh giúp dữ liệu di chuyển với tốc độ ánh sáng.

🚀 $FOTO (First Trust Indxx Global Photonics ETF) đầu tư vào các công ty hàng đầu trong lĩnh vực:

🔹 Truyền thông quang (Optical Networking)
🔹 Laser công suất cao
🔹 Silicon Photonics
🔹 Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học

🏆 Các khoản nắm giữ lớn nhất:
• Lumentum ($LITE) – Laser & module quang cho AI Datacenter
• IPG Photonics ($IPGP) – Laser công nghiệp hàng đầu thế giới
• Coherent ($COHR) – Chuỗi giá trị quang tử toàn diện
• Ciena ($CIEN) – Hạ tầng mạng quang tốc độ cao
• nLIGHT ($LASR) – Laser hiệu suất cao
• Fabrinet ($FN) – Nhà sản xuất module quang quy mô lớn
• AAOI ($AAOI) – Tích hợp dọc trong chuỗi cung ứng quang học

📊 Phân bổ ngành:
• 40.7% Thiết bị truyền thông quang & module
• 26.1% Laser
• 17.3% Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học
• 9.0% Gia công sản xuất quang học
• 6.9% Khác

🔥 Vì sao quang tử là chủ đề đáng chú ý?

✅ AI tạo ra lượng dữ liệu khổng lồ
✅ GPU ngày càng mạnh nhưng kết nối đang trở thành nút thắt cổ chai
✅ Silicon Photonics giúp tăng băng thông và giảm điện năng tiêu thụ
✅ Các ông lớn AI đang đẩy mạnh đầu tư vào hạ tầng quang học cho Datacenter thế hệ mới

🎯 Nếu NVIDIA là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh kết nối toàn bộ cơ thể AI.

Sherry thấy đây là một trong những chủ đề hạ tầng AI đáng theo dõi nhất trong giai đoạn 2025–2030 khi nhu cầu truyền dữ liệu tốc độ cao tiếp tục bùng nổ.

📌 Nghiên cứu kỹ trước khi đầu tư. Bài viết chỉ mang tính chất tham khảo, không phải khuyến nghị mua bán.
Sherry Lab 🚀

text:
Hey chat, let me put some respect on $ALRIB 🧵

Riber SA. French semi equipment maker. ~€300M market cap. 109 employees. global leader in MBE — the process used to grow semiconductor layers inside quantum dot lasers, photonics chips, and AI data center components.

You've never heard of them. that's the point.
The numbers→ revenue €40.3M, net income €5.2M, margin 13% → order book up 24% to €26.9M end of 2025 → fresh Japan production order in Jan 2026 (not even in the book yet)

ROSIE is the real thesis - Their new platform for silicon-based integrated photonics, compatible with 300mm production lines. first two units ordered in 2025. ROSIE 2 (dual-chamber) in production in 2026.

This is the format hyperscalers need. 300mm compatibility = plug into existing fab infrastructure. that's not a research tool. that's an industrial product entering a massive market.

Why it's undervalued - Every quantum dot laser in every AI data center optical link needs MBE - Riber is the global leader in that equipment. 
One company : 109 people, €300M market cap. analyst target still €6. stock at €15.70. analysts haven't moved since the AI photonics cycle started.

Upcoming catalyst June 17 shareholder meeting. €0.10/share cash distribution (+25%) proposed, payable June 24. Profitable company returning cash while deploying next-gen tech. 

This is what undervalued looks like before the market figures it out.

text:
US AFTERMARKET + MACRO RISK SCAN — June 4, 2026
Matrix Analyst AI | Pulatov Matrix Framework
OVERVIEW: THE SCHIZOPHRENIC MARKET
The US market is caught in the most vivid illustration of the matrix's central thesis: two formational forces pulling simultaneously in opposite directions. On one side — Nvidia, valued at $5 trillion, trading around $221–232 range intraday on June 3  (Robinhood) , with the AI/compute mega-cycle accelerating. On the other — WTI futures at $96.02 and Brent at $97.81, rising as the US and Iran traded new strikes, with the Strait of Hormuz still partially blocked  (CNBC) . This is not a "mixed market." This is the rodovye skhvatki formatsii — the birth pangs of the transition, playing out in real time on the ticker.
OLD FORMATION COLUMN: THERMAL COMBUSTION UNDER SIEGE
The thermal energy base of late-stage Capitalism is experiencing its most acute stress test since 2008. Roughly 14 million barrels per day of oil supply remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with around 850 million barrels of cumulative supply lost over the first two months of the conflict  (ING THINK) . This is not a "geopolitical risk premium" — this is a structural rupture in the energy column of the old formational matrix.
Brent peaked at $138 per barrel in April — the highest since 2008 — before pulling back to the mid-$90s on ceasefire optimism, creating a violent $90–$100 range with a $138 tail-risk scenario if the strait fully closes  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) . Saudi Aramco's CEO has reportedly warned the market won't normalize until 2027 if the strait stays shut past mid-June. Capital is pricing a reopening that hasn't happened. That is the definition of fragile.
The Democracy-Capitalist subject is responding predictably: global oil output expected to fall 6.9 million barrels per day year-on-year in Q2 2026, the largest quarterly decline since COVID-19  (World Bank) . Professional union rights — the freedom level of the old formation — become irrelevant when thermal supply is collapsing. Inflation resets all social contracts.
NEW FORMATION COLUMN: AI/COMPUTE SIGNAL INTENSIFYING
While thermal combustion bleeds, the new productive force is printing record valuations. Nvidia at Computex announced Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot for academic research, DRIVE Hyperion as the global platform for a robotaxi-ready world, Alpamayo 2 Super Open Reasoning Model for robotaxis, and Cosmos 3 as the open frontier foundation model for physical AI  (CNN) — all in a single product cycle. This is not iteration. This is the embodied intelligence column of the new formational matrix going vertical.
Marvell Technology surged to $290.79, up 32%, after Nvidia CEO highlighted its AI infrastructure role  (Robinhood) . Marvell acquired Celestial AI — a provider of Photonic Fabric technology for next-generation scale-up interconnect — for $3.5 billion in February 2026, with the acquisition expected to accelerate connectivity strategy for next-generation AI and cloud data centers  (sec) . Photonics is the new energy efficiency layer: it sits in the "New energy / power management" column of the transition. This is not a trade — this is capital voting for the next formational architecture.
CONFLICTS OF TRANSITION
The matrix identifies three live collision zones right now:
First, thermal shock vs AI-boom dissociation. The same session saw oil near $96 and Nvidia near $232 all-time-high territory. The old formational capital (energy companies, industrial complex) is being taxed by the Hormuz crisis while new formational capital (AI, semiconductors, optics) breaks records. This is capital migrating between formations in real time.
Second, capital volatility as formational stress indicator. Brent ran from below $60 at the start of 2026 to a $138 April peak before the May unwind — a range that reflects the system repricing into an entirely different energy regime  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) .

text: Typical zionist manipulation today on photonics what a great day to buy more i added to my $aaoi position

2026 is shaping up to be the year of photonics and optical infrastructure. 🚀

🔹 $AAOI — Supplies optical transceivers powering AI data center connectivity. High-growth potential, but results can be volatile.

🔹 $AVGO — Dominates custom AI chips and networking hardware. One of the biggest beneficiaries of AI infrastructure spending.

🔹 $LITE — Leading provider of optical components for hyperscale data centers and telecom networks. Recovery story with AI tailwinds.

🔹 $CRDO — Builds high-speed connectivity solutions for AI clusters. Growing rapidly, though expectations are already sky-high.

🔹 $ALAB — Emerging player in optical networking silicon. Early innings with a massive addressable market ahead.

🔹 $GLW — The backbone behind fiber and specialty glass. Less flashy, but quietly benefits from the AI buildout.

🔹 $COHR — Photonics and laser technology leader. AI opportunity is significant, but recent integration challenges have masked the story.

🔹 $AEHR — Semiconductor testing specialist expanding beyond EVs into AI, GaN, and next-generation chip markets.

As AI scales, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as computing it. That’s where photonics comes in. 💡

text:
$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade.

"$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade."}}}, "rest_id": "2062265269427552310", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2001", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The headline is the trillion-dollar tag. 

Disaggregate the compute across the data center and connectivity becomes the product. Marvell is the architecture layer for that. Closer to Broadcom than Lumentum, exactly like you framed it.

What most are missing: this was not a free compliment. Nvidia put $2B into Marvell on March 31 through NVLink Fusion, with a silicon photonics pledge attached. Jensen was talking his own book on stage.

You called it not in the basket. I made the case for the other side. Full breakdown here:

https://t.co/yizg4rAIFH 

So here is the open question for the hoard. Does that $2B stake make Marvell a partner, or a captured supplier?

text:
Two Deep-Tech Innovators Presenting June 10: BIO-key International and Syntec Optics

Two very different technologies — biometric identity authentication and precision photonics — united by one theme: solving problems that require extreme accuracy in high-stakes environments.

BIO-key International (OTC: $BKYI) | 3:00 PM ET - A global leader in Identity and Access Management (IAM) and biometric authentication, serving defense, financial, and government customers across multiple continents.

Syntec Optics Holdings (Nasdaq: $OPTX) | 3:30 PM ET - One of the largest custom optics and photonics manufacturers in the United States, operating for over two decades out of its Rochester, NY facility.

Two companies. Two distinct markets. Both operating at the leading edge of their fields.

June 10, 2026 | Register free: https://t.co/F6isjDz8Ci

text:
🚨 Photonics may become the most important technology shift of the next decade.

Not AI software.

Not apps.

Not even GPUs alone.

The REAL revolution may be the replacement of electricity with LIGHT inside computing infrastructure. 🧵

Silicon electronics are approaching physical limits.

Modern AI infrastructure now faces: ⚠️ bandwidth ceilings ⚠️ power-consumption explosions ⚠️ heat-density problems ⚠️ latency bottlenecks ⚠️ interconnect saturation

The current architecture is becoming unsustainable.

This is where photonics enters.

Photonics = using LIGHT (photons) instead of electricity (electrons) to: 💡 transmit data 💡 process signals 💡 move information 💡 scale compute infrastructure

The implications are enormous.

We believe photonics is no longer speculative science fiction.

It is increasingly becoming: ⚠️ necessary ⚠️ commercially viable ⚠️ strategically essential

Especially for hyperscale AI systems.

The AI boom itself is creating the problem photonics solves.

As models scale: 📈 compute demand explodes 📈 rack density rises 📈 cooling loads surge 📈 electricity consumption skyrockets

Copper interconnects increasingly become the bottleneck.

Traditional electrical systems waste massive amounts of energy as heat.

Photonics changes the equation: ⚡ lower power consumption ⚡ faster transmission ⚡ lower latency ⚡ higher bandwidth ⚡ less thermal stress

This is why hyperscalers are aggressively pursuing it.

In this case the sequence is straightforward:

Step 1: 🤖 AI models scale exponentially

Step 2: ⚠️ Electrical interconnects hit limits

Step 3: 🔥 Cooling and power costs explode

Step 4: 💡 Photonics becomes economically necessary

Step 5: 🏗 Entire data-center architecture changes

This transition is already happening quietly.

Over the next 3–5 years: ⚠️ co-packaged optics ⚠️ optical switching ⚠️ silicon photonics ⚠️ photonic networking

…will increasingly replace traditional copper infrastructure.

The implications go far beyond performance.

Photonics may fundamentally reshape: 🏭 semiconductor design ⚡ power consumption 💧 cooling infrastructure 🧠 AI deployment economics 🏢 hyperscale construction

We believe this becomes one of the clearest examples of K-shaped technological consolidation.

Why?

Because only the largest players can afford: 💰 photonic R&D 💰 custom AI infrastructure 💰 advanced packaging 💰 optical integration (Watch chip makers rotating, new partnerships)

Smaller competitors increasingly get locked out.

The photonics transition favors: ✅ hyperscalers ✅ mega-cap semiconductor firms ✅ defense-linked infrastructure players ✅ vertically integrated ecosystems

The technological moat widens.

🚨 INVESTABLE PLAYERS

Several companies already sit directly inside this transition.

⚡ Broadcom ($AVGO) ⚡ Coherent ($COHR) ⚡ Lumentum ($LITE) ⚡ Intel ($INTC) ⚡ Laser Photonics ($LASE)

Each attacks a different layer of the stack.

$AVGO is one of the strongest large-cap photonics plays.

Broadcom already dominates: 🔌 networking 🔌 optical connectivity 🔌 silicon-photonics infrastructure 🔌 AI data-center interconnects

It sits directly at the AI bandwidth bottleneck.

$COHR is another critical player.

Coherent controls: 💡 industrial lasers 💡 optical subsystems 💡 photonic components 💡 semiconductor manufacturing optics

As photonic infrastructure scales, laser demand scales with it.

$LITE remains deeply leveraged to: 📡 fiber optics 📡 telecom photonics 📡 optical sensing 📡 AI networking expansion

The company effectively monetizes bandwidth growth itself.

$INTC is quietly positioning aggressively in silicon photonics.

Intel understands a critical reality:

Traditional scaling alone is no longer enough.

Future compute increasingly depends on: 💡 optical movement of data rather than purely electrical scaling.

Smaller pure-play names like: ⚡ $LASE ⚡ Ayar Labs (private) ⚡ Lightmatter (private)

represent the more speculative frontier.

Higher risk. Potentially enormous upside.

Especially if optical interconnect adoption accelerates faster than expected.

Emphasis

In photonics the bottlenecks are: ⚠️ optical integration ⚠️ laser systems ⚠️ advanced packaging ⚠️ thermal efficiency ⚠️ networking infrastructure

The defense angle matters enormously too.

Photonics is not just about AI chatbots.

It increasingly affects: 🛰 satellite systems 🎯 targeting infrastructure 📡 radar systems ⚔ electronic warfare 🤖 autonomous drones

This is becoming strategic infrastructure.

Another major theme:

Photonics helps reduce energy intensity.

This becomes critical because hyperscale AI now collides directly with: ⚡ grid shortages 💧 water scarcity 🔥 cooling constraints 🛢 diesel stress

The physical economy increasingly constrains digital expansion.

We have repeatedly emphasized:

The future AI race may not simply be about: “Who has the best model?”

It may become: “Who can physically power and cool computation?”

Photonics helps solve that problem.

This is why the market increasingly rewards: 🏗 infrastructure ⚡ utilities 💧 cooling 🧲 critical minerals 💡 photonic efficiency

The AI story is becoming deeply industrial.

The broader implication is profound:

Photonics may ultimately do to traditional electronics… …what the internet did to analog communication.

The infrastructure layer itself changes.

But the transition also accelerates concentration.

Only a narrowing circle of: 🏢 hyperscalers 🏦 mega-cap firms 🏭 industrial suppliers ⚔ defense-linked infrastructure players

…may fully benefit.

The K-shape deepens.

The irony is striking:

The AI revolution was originally marketed as democratizing technology.

Instead it increasingly centralizes: ⚡ compute ⚡ infrastructure ⚡ capital ⚡ energy access ⚡ photonic capability

into fewer hands.

We believe the market still underestimates how quickly photonics may become unavoidable.

Because the problem is no longer theoretical.

The electrical system itself is hitting physical limits.

The final takeaway:

The next generation of AI winners may not simply be software companies.

They may be the firms controlling: 💡 light transmission ⚡ power efficiency 🔥 thermal management 🏗 optical infrastructure

The future of computing increasingly belongs to photonics.

{"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "🚨 Photonics may become the most important technology shift of the next decade. Not AI software. Not apps. Not even GPUs alone. The REAL revolution may be the replacement of electricity with LIGHT inside computing infrastructure. 🧵 Silicon electronics are approaching physical limits. Modern AI infrastructure now faces: ⚠️ bandwidth ceilings ⚠️ power-consumption explosions ⚠️ heat-density problems ⚠️ latency bottlenecks ⚠️ interconnect saturation The current architecture is becoming unsustainable. This is where photonics enters. Photonics = using LIGHT (photons) instead of electricity (electrons) to: 💡 transmit data 💡 process signals 💡 move information 💡 scale compute infrastructure The implications are enormous. We believe photonics is no longer speculative science fiction. It is increasingly becoming: ⚠️ necessary ⚠️ commercially viable ⚠️ strategically essential Especially for hyperscale AI systems. The AI boom itself is creating the problem photonics solves. As models scale: 📈 compute demand explodes 📈 rack density rises 📈 cooling loads surge 📈 electricity consumption skyrockets Copper interconnects increasingly become the bottleneck. Traditional electrical systems waste massive amounts of energy as heat. Photonics changes the equation: ⚡ lower power consumption ⚡ faster transmission ⚡ lower latency ⚡ higher bandwidth ⚡ less thermal stress This is why hyperscalers are aggressively pursuing it. In this case the sequence is straightforward: Step 1: 🤖 AI models scale exponentially Step 2: ⚠️ Electrical interconnects hit limits Step 3: 🔥 Cooling and power costs explode Step 4: 💡 Photonics becomes economically necessary Step 5: 🏗 Entire data-center architecture changes This transition is already happening quietly. Over the next 3–5 years: ⚠️ co-packaged optics ⚠️ optical switching ⚠️ silicon photonics ⚠️ photonic networking …will increasingly replace traditional copper infrastructure. The implications go far beyond performance. Photonics may fundamentally reshape: 🏭 semiconductor design ⚡ power consumption 💧 cooling infrastructure 🧠 AI deployment economics 🏢 hyperscale construction We believe this becomes one of the clearest examples of K-shaped technological consolidation. Why? Because only the largest players can afford: 💰 photonic R&D 💰 custom AI infrastructure 💰 advanced packaging 💰 optical integration (Watch chip makers rotating, new partnerships) Smaller competitors increasingly get locked out. The photonics transition favors: ✅ hyperscalers ✅ mega-cap semiconductor firms ✅ defense-linked infrastructure players ✅ vertically integrated ecosystems The technological moat widens. 🚨 INVESTABLE PLAYERS Several companies already sit directly inside this transition. ⚡ Broadcom ($AVGO) ⚡ Coherent ($COHR) ⚡ Lumentum ($LITE) ⚡ Intel ($INTC) ⚡ Laser Photonics ($LASE) Each attacks a different layer of the stack. $AVGO is one of the strongest large-cap photonics plays. Broadcom already dominates: 🔌 networking 🔌 optical connectivity 🔌 silicon-photonics infrastructure 🔌 AI data-center interconnects It sits directly at the AI bandwidth bottleneck. $COHR is another critical player. Coherent controls: 💡 industrial lasers 💡 optical subsystems 💡 photonic components 💡 semiconductor manufacturing optics As photonic infrastructure scales, laser demand scales with it. $LITE remains deeply leveraged to: 📡 fiber optics 📡 telecom photonics 📡 optical sensing 📡 AI networking expansion The company effectively monetizes bandwidth growth itself. $INTC is quietly positioning aggressively in silicon photonics. Intel understands a critical reality: Traditional scaling alone is no longer enough. Future compute increasingly depends on: 💡 optical movement of data rather than purely electrical scaling. Smaller pure-play names like: ⚡ $LASE ⚡ Ayar Labs (private) ⚡ Lightmatter (private) represent the more speculative frontier. Higher risk. Potentially enormous upside. Especially if optical interconnect adoption accelerates faster than expected. Emphasis In photonics the bottlenecks are: ⚠️ optical integration ⚠️ laser systems ⚠️ advanced packaging ⚠️ thermal efficiency ⚠️ networking infrastructure The defense angle matters enormously too. Photonics is not just about AI chatbots. It increasingly affects: 🛰 satellite systems 🎯 targeting infrastructure 📡 radar systems ⚔ electronic warfare 🤖 autonomous drones This is becoming strategic infrastructure. Another major theme: Photonics helps reduce energy intensity. This becomes critical because hyperscale AI now collides directly with: ⚡ grid shortages 💧 water scarcity 🔥 cooling constraints 🛢 diesel stress The physical economy increasingly constrains digital expansion. We have repeatedly emphasized: The future AI race may not simply be about: “Who has the best model?” It may become: “Who can physically power and cool computation?” Photonics helps solve that problem. This is why the market increasingly rewards: 🏗 infrastructure ⚡ utilities 💧 cooling 🧲 critical minerals 💡 photonic efficiency The AI story is becoming deeply industrial. The broader implication is profound: Photonics may ultimately do to traditional electronics… …what the internet did to analog communication. The infrastructure layer itself changes. But the transition also accelerates concentration. Only a narrowing circle of: 🏢 hyperscalers 🏦 mega-cap firms 🏭 industrial suppliers ⚔ defense-linked infrastructure players …may fully benefit. The K-shape deepens. The irony is striking: The AI revolution was originally marketed as democratizing technology. Instead it increasingly centralizes: ⚡ compute ⚡ infrastructure ⚡ capital ⚡ energy access ⚡ photonic capability into fewer hands. We believe the market still underestimates how quickly photonics may become unavoidable. Because the problem is no longer theoretical. The electrical system itself is hitting physical limits. The final takeaway: The next generation of AI winners may not simply be software companies. They may be the firms controlling: 💡 light transmission ⚡ power efficiency 🔥 thermal management 🏗 optical infrastructure The future of computing increasingly belongs to photonics. #Photonics #AI #Semiconductors #SiliconPhotonics #DataCenters #Broadcom #AVGO #Coherent #COHR #Lumentum #LITE #Intel #LASE #QuantumComputing #Optics #Hyperscalers #Infrastructure #ArtificialIntelligence #Computing #CriticalMinerals #EnergyCrisis #KShapedEconomy #Investing #StockMarket #TechStocks #PiggosTradingDesk @DonMiami3 @Whitelavender92 @Diablo_Blanco_3 @Marbella_Media"}}}, "rest_id": "2062948497977196803", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "143", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
============================================================
SOLIDIFICATION OF LIGHT AND PROGRAMMABLE MATTER
Connection Brief
============================================================

Solidification of Light:
- Refers to experiments making photons behave like condensed matter.
- Key breakthrough (March 2025): Italian researchers created the first
  "photonic supersolid" — light that has a rigid crystalline structure
  (solid-like) while flowing without friction (superfluid-like).
- Achieved using exciton-polaritons in semiconductor nanostructures.

Programmable Matter:
- Materials that can change physical properties (shape, density,
  optical traits, stiffness) in a controlled/programmable way.
- Includes metamaterials, shape-changing systems, and modular robotics.

Connections:
1. Light as a programmable medium: Supersolid light and polariton
   systems allow dynamic, reconfigurable quantum states controlled
   by lasers or external fields.

2. Programmable photonics overlap: Solid-light phenomena can serve
   as building blocks for adaptive optical chips, tunable lasers,
   optical neural networks, and quantum simulators.

3. Hybrid future tech: Combines exotic quantum behavior of "solid"
   light with the reconfigurability of smart materials, enabling
   potential applications in quantum computing, adaptive optics,
   and light-based programmable devices.

Current status: Mostly laboratory-scale quantum experiments, but
strong conceptual bridge toward future smart photonic materials.

References:
[1] Live Science / Nature (2025) - Photonic supersolid creation
[2] Wikipedia / ScienceAlert - Solid light and supersolids
[3] Wikipedia - Programmable matter
[4] Related works on polaritons and programmable photonics

text:
Researchers at Monash University have developed a tiny chip that could help pave the way for computers that process information using light instead of electricity. Published in the journal Nature Photonics, the study demonstrates a nanoscale device capable of generating, controlling and reading light-based signals on a single platform. The breakthrough uses an emerging technology called valleytronics, which relies on quantum properties in advanced materials to encode information. Researchers say the innovation could lead to faster computing, lower energy consumption and more efficient communication systems in the future.

Investigadores de la Universidad Monash desarrollaron un diminuto chip que podría ayudar a abrir el camino hacia computadoras capaces de procesar información utilizando luz en lugar de electricidad. Publicado en la revista Nature Photonics, el estudio demuestra un dispositivo a escala nanométrica capaz de generar, controlar y leer señales basadas en luz en una sola plataforma. El avance utiliza una tecnología emergente conocida como valleytrónica, que aprovecha propiedades cuánticas de materiales avanzados para codificar información. Los investigadores afirman que la innovación podría conducir a una computación más rápida, un menor consumo de energía y sistemas de comunicación más eficientes en el futuro.

#Chip #Information #Light #TechInvestigation #valleytronics

text:
Karnataka CM @DKShivakumar on Friday met a high-level delegation from the German state of Thuringia, led by Minister-President Mario Voigt, to discuss strengthening economic ties and investment opportunities. 

Heavy Industries Minister @MBPatil said Karnataka and Thuringia can collaborate in optics, photonics, semiconductors, biotech, EV manufacturing, renewable energy and advanced manufacturing. Shivakumar highlighted opportunities in technology transfer, innovation, skill exchange and next-generation technologies. Voigt praised Karnataka’s R&D ecosystem, startup network, engineering talent, quantum technology & aerospace infrastructure.

Photonics bottlenecks go way beyond lasers. 

CPO/NPO integration, thermal mgmt, and OCS switches are key scalers. Diversify across the chain for AI cluster exposure. 

Pluggables probably start ramping in 2027, CPO follows. High TAM but watch yields, hyperscaler quals & execution risks. 

Picks & shovels win regardless.

text:
While Nasdaq is down 3%, the number one gainer on the S&P today is FedEx Freight $FDXF at +9%.

But, no one cared about this 5 days ago, because it's not related to bottlenecks in photonics.

Yet, trucking - especially LTL - is a bottleneck. Plus, the re-rating vs. $ODFL, $SAIA, and $XPO after spin.

Though, it's too boring for X, and such posts are becoming increasingly a waste of time.

This was #1 reason I've kept $FDX but sold my $UPS couple years ago. The theoretical value of the distributed stake implied an initial $FDXF price in the mid-to-high $170s range. As typical with spin-offs, they get sold and right now we're $150s. Estimated FY2026 revenue: $8.7B (largest North American LTL carrier by revenue). It carries major debt due to a $4.1B cash dividend paid to FedEx pre-spin. Old Dominion $ODFL: EV/EBITDA 25–27x, PE 43–47x. Superior operating ratio 72–75% range historically/recently and consistent execution. Saia $SAIA and $XPO: Also premium high 40+ PE, strong EV/EBITDA, with growth/margin stories. Potential $FDFX catalysts: - Immediate S&P 500 and Dow Jones Transportation Average inclusion (index buying). - Pure-play LTL focus vs. being buried inside $FDX . - Potential for margin/ROIC improvement under dedicated management and capex/IT focus. -Long-term re-rating opportunity if it narrows the efficiency gap toward $ODFL $SAIA levels. I added 50 shares simply because the 50 I got is too small. Similar to a semiconductor IPO, hard to think of a better time in recent history to do a trucking IPO/spinoff."}}}, "rest_id": "2061539530730254635", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15275", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062943437528404282", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10755", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
PhotonVentures is a European venture capital fund dedicated to integrated photonics, investing in companies from seed to Series A and supporting their transition from early validation to scalable businesses. As a specialized deep tech investor, the fund combines capital with direct access to a global ecosystem of foundries, designers, and system integrators via PhotonDelta.

This article outlines how investors evaluate early-stage photonic chip startups, what differentiates investment-ready teams, and how companies can position themselves for scale, drawing on the perspective of PhotonVentures. It focuses on the practical signals investors look for across technology, market readiness, and ecosystem alignment.

Full interview here: https://t.co/HKQNA1CSJP

#engineering #technology #photonics #PICs

@photondelta

4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. The chain locks together: stress on any single link reprices every link above it."}}}, "rest_id": "2062502220889837836", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "265485", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062936149908213920", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "41", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
An investor with over 7 years of experience as a $SIVE shareholder

Maintains a clearly optimistic view on the future of the company

Highlights its strategic positioning in cutting edge technologies such as:

- Integrated photonics

- Optical solutions for AI data centers

- High-performance wireless communications

- Defense applications

The investor considers that recent criticism appearing in Swedish media

Short seller reports

And share sales by former executives represent temporary "noise" rather than anything else

Which does not reflect the company's fundamental reality

In their opinion, these negative elements overshadow significant achievements

Like important strategic alliances:

- Partnership with $GFS

- Ayar Labs entering the $NVDA ecosystem

- Inclusion in the MSCI Small Caps

- Nasdaq dual listing

And a solid pipeline of opportunities hovering around $800M

With 77% growth in a quarter

With the global AI boom as a backdrop

And backed by external validations such as CHIPS Act funding

The $SIVE shareholder invites interested parties to do their own research

Reminding that strong convictions often feel lonely before becoming obvious to the market

I couldn't agree more with their opinion

Bullish as ever on $SIVE

1601], "text": "AAPL"}], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjMyNTE5NDM5ODQ2MDcyMzI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Reflections from a Seven-Year Shareholder: Why Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVE Was Always Destined for Greatness After nearly seven years as a shareholder in Sivers Semiconductors, I have watched this company evolve from an overlooked Swedish deeptech player into a force at the intersection of photonics, wireless communications, AI infrastructure, and strategic defense technologies. The recent surge in the share price is not a surprise to those of us who have followed the story closely it is the logical unfolding of a thesis many of us articulated years ago when few were listening. From the moment I first dug into the company, its potential felt enormous and profoundly underappreciated. The technology advanced lasers for co-packaged optics in AI data centers, beamforming ICs for 5G/6G and SATCOM, and full-duplex arrays for electronic warfare sits at the heart of multiple secular megatrends. Yet for years, the market fixated on quarterly losses, development costs, and execution risks while largely ignoring the customer pipeline, partnerships, and technological edge. Where Unicorns Are Born This is how real winners emerge. History is clear: transformative companies are rarely discovered in consensus comfort zones. They are unearthed where sentiment is exhausted, where weak hands have capitulated, and where the narrative is dominated by skepticism. Sivers spent years in that crucible not for the faint-hearted. You had to immerse yourself in the details: the photonics platform’s unique indium phosphide capabilities and its work with hyperscalers, AI data center players, $AAPL (sensing) and Win Semiconductors, along with key partners such as Jabil and GlobalFoundries; the wireless division’s tier-1 engagements with names like Nokia, BAE Systems, SATCOM operators and the U.S. government itself. Companies and institutions of that caliber do not collaborate, co-develop, or plan multi-year programs with you unless there is real substance and a clear path forward. That, for me, is the only validation you truly need. Add to that the expanding opportunity pipeline now approaching $800 million and strategic validations like repeat US CHIPS Act funding, and the picture becomes very clear. Bears and headline-chasers thrive on the opposite approach. Swedish media have produced more than 50 negative articles in recent times the positive ones you can count on one hand. It is a witch hunt. They obsessively highlight risk, risk, risk, with almost no mention of the enormous potential. How can anyone take them seriously? They have no skin in the game. If they could invest successfully, they wouldn’t be working for newspapers. They pretend to be all-knowing, but the truth is no one knows exactly where technology and the market are heading. After two decades working with IT companies large and small I understand both the tech and the market. That is why I am bullish, and why this development does not surprise me. The same goes for the short sellers. One recent 43-page “analysis” packed with incorrect assumptions and misinformation was clearly designed to create fear and doubt. I could refute every single point, but why give them more oxygen? Notably, they don’t even dare to hold a meaningful disclosed short position. That is weaker than their analysis. I put real money on the table and take real risk. Please increase your shorts. I am waiting for you to go under. Leadership and Shareholder Transitions: A Healthy Reset Some former insiders and larger holders have exited. I am not surprised I am relieved. They did not deserve to be part of the journey that is now unfolding, a journey that is still far from over. Erik Fällström and his associates supported the company for many years, and for that we are grateful. But the attempts to extract personal gain crossed a line most notably trying to spin off the photonics division into a SPAC where he (via Achilles Capital / DDM) was a major sponsor, at what looked like bargain terms. The chairman and new CEO rightly put a stop to it. Shortly after, the selling began. He sold the majority of his holdings around 4 SEK. Karma is real. In parallel, Achilles Capital and its parent DDM Finance have been forced to apply for corporate reconstruction due to massive debt issues. The contrast between opportunism and long-term conviction could not be clearer. Harish Krishnaswamy came in via the MixComm acquisition. I will not lie I like Harish. He is technically strong and has an excellent network that helped land key development agreements, including CHIPS Act wins. At the same time, I am not surprised he is selling. He has sold multiple times before, often at 4–8 SEK levels. This latest sale seems to be his chance to redeem all the earlier exits at much lower prices a kind of psychological average to finally make it feel better. Am I happy about it? No. But this is the same person who, several times right after Sivers secured major agreements, sold and killed the momentum, or sold when tax bills came due. As one of the founders, I think he simply wanted to feel that his years at MixComm finally delivered something tangible. This was the last time, and frankly, it feels damn good. Funds that fully exited did so because they operate under strict risk mandates and manage other people’s money. That is natural and not a negative signal. These departures represent a healthy cleansing. The right people and the right long-term capital are now aligned for the next phase. The Path Forward No one has a crystal ball. No one knows exactly how large the Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) market will ultimately become. But I genuinely believe people will be shocked by the speed and momentum once it really starts ramping. The combination of exploding AI compute demand, power constraints in data centers, and Sivers’ differentiated indium phosphide laser platform positions the company at the center of one of the most important technology shifts of this decade. The current momentum validates what patient shareholders have long seen. Product ramps, pipeline conversion, CHIPS Act milestones, and potential US dual-listing preparations are tangible progress. Volatility will remain; bears will resurface. But conviction built on deep research outlasts noise. To newer investors: Do your own work. Ignore the echo chamber of fear. Build your own mosaic from primary sources. True edges come from independent thinking. The easy path is skepticism and short-termism. The harder, more rewarding one is sustained belief grounded in analysis. Sivers was never a quick flip. It was and remains a multi-year compounder for those willing to look beyond the noise. The diamond was always there for those with the eyes to see it. The journey is far from over, and the best chapters are still ahead. Stay bullish. Stay informed. And above all, trust the work you’ve done."}}}, "rest_id": "2063251944550838728", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "231619", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063366985308852538", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7679", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
TSMC’s COUPE™ platform is one to watch.
It brings #siliconphotonics into the advanced packaging stack: combining CoWoS®, SoIC®, advanced logic, HBM, interposers, embedded components, and optical engine in one HPC platform for AI.
This is the technology foundation underpinning  NVIDIA’s CPO roadmap: moving optical I/O closer to the compute fabric to unlock higher bandwidth, lower latency, and better power efficiency for next-gen AI clusters.
Silicon photonics is not just a networking add-on. It is becoming 
is becoming part of the AI packaging stack.

Sense → Edge inference → Connectivity → Humanoid.

All 4 layer sit on top of the foundational layer.
